methodology: 13 finder lenses swept the codebase (rsi .p I/O and numeric conversion; epsilon physics; chi physics; scor160/ATOMIX benchmark; instrument-agnostic processing incl. trim/bottom/ct_align/mixing; perturb campaign pipeline; config/hashing/incremental-cache infrastructure; perturb plotting; pyturb; documentation self-consistency; through-water speed / fall-rate; ancillary-data (hotel/GPS/CTD/seawater) ingestion; dual-N2 stratification), plus a dedicated fix-status phase re-verifying the 24 prior-audit findings tracked across PRs #79-#87. Raw candidates were semantically de-duplicated (merged where a defect and its coverage/trigger sibling coincided). Survivors then went through batched adversarial refute-by-default verification: each verifier agent judged a small GROUP of findings (majors in groups of 3; minors/nits in groups of 6) within one context, applying all three perspectives per finding (reproduce: run the repro; reference: check against ODAS MATLAB / published physics; context: assess default-path reachability and prior-audit overlap). A finding is CONFIRMED only if the verifier did not refute it; verifier-recalibrated severity overrides the finder's. A completeness-critic round 2 covered gaps. NOTE ON GATE STRENGTH: verification was BATCHED (one agent per group) rather than one-independent-agent-per-lens, an adaptation forced by usage-window budget limits; this is a slightly weaker adversarial gate than independent per-lens voting and is disclosed honestly. Unverified findings (verifier failed to return a verdict) are neither confirmed nor refuted; there were none this round. Baseline at HEAD with PYTHONPATH=/Users/pat/tpw/tpw/turbulence/src: 2154 passed, 76 skipped, 0 failed.

confirmed_findings.title: dTdz fits in-situ temperature, so the adiabatic lapse rate (~2.4e-4 K/m in ARCTERX warm water) systematically biases K_T and Gamma by 1.3x-11x (unbounded) in weak-gradient regimes and defeats the 1e-4 K/m well-mixed mask
confirmed_findings.claim: Because _stable_window (mixing.py:327) and window_stratification (mixing.py:203) fit the least-squares slope of IN-SITU temperature vs depth, the background gradient feeding K_T = chi/(2 dTdz^2) and Gamma = N2*chi/(2 eps dTdz^2) includes the adiabatic lapse rate (2.42e-4 K/m at T=29 C, S=34.5 — 2.4x the DEFAULT_DTDZ_MIN=1e-4 floor), so on the default path of BOTH pipelines K_T/Gamma are overestimated x1.76 at true dCT/dz=1e-3 K/m, x3.8 at 5e-4, x10.9 at 3.5e-4 (unbounded near -2.4e-4 where the in-situ slope cancels), underestimated x0.45 for a +5e-4 inversion, and valid weakly-stratified windows with true |dCT/dz| ~1.4e-4 to 3.4e-4 are silently masked as well-mixed.
confirmed_findings.evidence: mixing.py:327 dTdz = polyfit(z_stable, T_stable) fits in-situ Tw, while the SAME function computes adiabatically-correct CT_stable (mixing.py:324) and uses it only for N2 (:334-335) — within one window N2 is lapse-corrected but dTdz^2 is not, making Gamma's numerator/denominator physically inconsistent. Consumed by rsi/pipeline.py:521-546 and perturb/pipeline.py:750,885. Measured with gsw at Saipan (SP=34.5, lat 15.2): lapse=2.416e-4 K/dbar at T=29/P=10; sorted_stratification repro: true dCT/dz=-1e-3 -> measured -7.54e-4 (bias x1.76); -5e-4 -> -2.56e-4 (x3.82); -3.5e-4 -> -1.06e-4 (x10.9); -2.4e-4 -> +3.6e-6 (MASKED though stratified). Physics: variance production is against d(theta)/dz (Osborn & Cox 1972); chi itself unaffected.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: cd scratchpad && PYTHONPATH=/Users/pat/tpw/tpw/turbulence/src python3 repro_insitu_dtdz.py — uniform-CT warm column shows in-situ dT=+2.4 mK over 10 dbar; weak-gradient case yields measured dTdz=+3.6e-6 (cancellation). Bias table over CT gradients -5e-3..+5e-4 confirms (g/measured)^2 of 1.11/1.30/1.76/3.82/10.9 and MASKED rows. Read mixing.py:319-336.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Fit the gradient of the already-computed conservative temperature in _stable_window and window_stratification; update dTdz comments/docs; add a regression test with a uniform-CT warm column asserting |dTdz| < DEFAULT_DTDZ_MIN.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed. _stable_window fits in-situ T for dTdz (mixing.py:327: polyfit(z_stable,T_stable)) but CT for N2 (:334-335) — the SAME window's Gamma=N2*chi/(2*eps*dTdz^2) mixes lapse-corrected numerator with uncorrected denominator. gsw at ARCTERX (SP=34.5,lat15.2,T=29C,P=10): adiabatic lapse=2.416e-4 K/dbar (matches finding); uniform-CT column has dT_insitu/dp=+2.416e-4. So dT_insitu/dz=dCT/dz+lapse. Verified K_T bias=(dCT/dTins)^2: 1.10x at dCT=-5e-3, 1.29x at -2e-3, 1.74x at -1e-3, 3.74x at -5e-4, 10.4x at -3.5e-4; MASKED (|ins|<floor) at -2.4e-4; 0.45x at +5e-4. lapse 2.4e-4 > DEFAULT_DTDZ_MIN=1e-4 (mixing.py:79), defeating the well-mixed mask (:498). Default path both pipelines: rsi/pipeline.py:521,546; perturb/pipeline.py:750,827. Osborn-Cox requires the conserved-scalar (potential/CT) gradient, so in-situ systematically overestimates K_T/Gamma (10-30% even in moderate thermoclines). Not in prior audits. Major: systematic wrong K_T/Gamma on realistic default-path inputs.

confirmed_findings.title: top_trim deepest-elevated-bin rule turns isolated deep accelerometer transients into 5-22 m over-trims, silently discarding valid near-surface epsilon/chi data on ~3.7% of real ARCTERX casts
confirmed_findings.claim: compute_trim_depth (top_trim.py:150-162) takes the DEEPEST bin with std > noise_factor*background as the prop-wash exit, so any isolated deep accelerometer transient (cable snap-load, 10-78x background on both Ax and Ay) inside the search range causes the entire quiet band above it to be trimmed; on the 29 real ARCTERX .p files with shipped campaign settings (top_trim.enable=true, max_depth=25) this over-trims 17/461 profiles (3.7%) by >5 m beyond the contiguous surface-wash exit, up to +22.25 m, ~446 m of cast discarded campaign-wide.
confirmed_findings.evidence: top_trim.py:157 'deepest = int(elevated_idx[-1])' with no contiguity-with-surface requirement. ARCTERX_Thompson_2025_SN479_0026.p prof 8: Ax/Ay quiet from 3-24.5 dbar, isolated transients at 25-33 and 49.5-50 dbar reach 17.6-78x background on BOTH axes (median combine cannot reject). End-to-end via perturb/pipeline.py:512-521 with campaign settings: 'prof8: top_trim pushed start 1.14 -> 25.01 dbar' — quiet 2.75-24.5 dbar dropped while contaminated 25-33 dbar RETAINED. Campaign survey: excess over contiguous-run exit p95 4.67 m, max 22.25 m; 17 profiles >5 m. With code defaults (max_depth=50): 14/461 trims >15 m, two at 48.5-50 m whose surface-run exits are 2.25-2.75 m. The audit #66 resolution note (2026-06-26-multiround-verified.md:65) records only 'trims settle at ~5 m (p90 9 m)', never this heavy tail — the unrecorded flip side of the deepest-elevated-bin fix.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: PYTHONPATH=/Users/pat/tpw/tpw/turbulence/src python3: load VMP/ARCTERX_Thompson_2025_SN479_0026.p, get_profiles, then _adjust_profile_bounds with top={'enable':True,'dz':0.5,'min_depth':1.0,'max_depth':25.0,'quantile':0.6}; observe prof8 start 1.14 -> 25.01 dbar. Bin Ax/Ay per-bin std via top_trim._bin_std: elevated bins only at 1-2.5, 25-33, 49.5-50 dbar.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Locate the prop-wash exit as the end of the elevated run CONTIGUOUS with the surface (allowing gaps up to k~2-4 bins, still fixing the audit-#66 momentary-lull case); log/flag isolated deep elevated bins separately as mid-column contamination.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed on real data. top_trim.py:157 (deepest=elevated_idx[-1]) has no surface-contiguity requirement, so an isolated deep bin sets the exit. Repro on VMP/...SN479_0026.p with campaign settings (ARCTERX/perturb.yaml: enable=true,max_depth=25,quantile=0.6): prof8 Ax elevated bin-centers=[1.0,1.5,2.0,2.5,25.0], Ay=[1.0,1.5,2.0,25.0] — contiguous surface wash 1-2.5m, quiet 3-24.5m gap, then an isolated elevated bin at the last search bin 25.0m; trim pushes start 1.14->25.01 dbar, discarding valid settled 2.75-24.5m water. Campaign survey over 464 real profiles: excess over contiguous-surface exit p95=5.25m, max=22.25m (exact match to finding; prof8 0026 surf_exit=2.75,trim=25.00), 24/464=5.2% over-trim >5m, 4 over 15m, ~598m discarded (finding's 3.7%/446m is conservative). Distinct from #66 (2026-06-26:65 records only the opposite under-trim and 'settle ~5m p90 9m'; the over-trim tail introduced by the #66 fix is unrecorded). Major: silent loss of valid near-surface epsilon/chi data on the default path at a realistic rate.

confirmed_findings.title: detect_bottom_crash flags mid-column vibration transients as bottom crashes (no bottom-proximity or speed-drop confirmation); on real ARCTERX data 6 of 12 detections are false positives whose caller truncation silently deletes 94-178 m of valid deep data
confirmed_findings.claim: detect_bottom_crash (bottom.py:141-153) returns the deepest depth-bin whose accel-magnitude std exceeds vibration_factor*median with no check that the flagged bin is near the cast bottom and with the MATLAB speed-drop confirmation explicitly unimplemented (bottom.py:52-55); run with defaults on all 464 real ARCTERX profiles it detects 12 crashes of which 6 are flagged at 11.9-140.0 dbar on casts that keep descending to 189-234 dbar, and the caller (perturb/pipeline.py:550-556) truncates 93.6-178.4 m of valid deep cast per false positive.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Survey (defaults, {Ax,Ay}, fs=512): 12/464 detections; 6 plausibly real (flagged 0.1-0.4 m above nanmax(depth), files 0005/0010/0028), 6 false: 0009 prof7 flagged 11.9 dbar on a 190.4 dbar cast (cuts 178.4 m), 0009 prof6 20.0/189.1, 0026 prof9 47.9/225.1, prof8 52.0/223.0, prof3 100.0/233.3, prof4 140.0/233.5. A profiler cannot strike bottom and keep falling 90-178 m. End-to-end with bottom.enable=true: 'prof3: bottom pushed end 233.30 -> 100.00 dbar' with only INFO logging. Realistic: shipped ARCTERX/perturb.yaml instructs 'Enable if any profile is suspected of striking bottom' and this campaign has 6 apparent real strikes. Prior audits recorded only M4 bin-center overhang (fixed), #63 half-bin under-trim, and #24/#67 warning leak — not this false-positive class.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: PYTHONPATH=/Users/pat/tpw/tpw/turbulence/src python3: for files 0009/0026, get_profiles then detect_bottom_crash per profile with fast-sliced Ax/Ay; expect 0009 prof6/7 flagged 20.0/11.9 dbar (casts ~189/190) and 0026 prof3/4/8/9 flagged 100/140/52/48 dbar (casts 223-234). Then _adjust_profile_bounds on 0026 with bottom enable shows 'bottom pushed end 233.30 -> 100.00 dbar'.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Require the flagged bin to be within N bins (~2) of the deepest populated bin, and/or implement the documented speed_factor confirmation (fall rate collapses at a real crash); warn (not INFO) when flagged depth is far above nanmax(depth).

verdict.reasoning: Mechanism confirmed by code + synthetic repro. detect_bottom_crash (bottom.py:141-153) returns the deepest depth-bin whose accel-mag std exceeds vibration_factor*median (default 4.0), with NO bottom-proximity gate; the MATLAB speed-drop confirmation is explicitly unimplemented (bottom.py:52-55). Synthetic cast descending smoothly to 234 dbar with a single mid-column vibration burst at ~100 dbar (quiet below) → detect_bottom_crash returned 99.9998 dbar. Caller (pipeline.py:550-563) sets new_e via `below=where(P_seg>=bottom_depth)`, which is non-empty here, so it truncates ~134 m of valid deep cast; only INFO logging. NOT a prior dup: M4/#63 were the OPPOSITE (bin-center overhang → UNDER-trim, now fixed to sample-mean). NOT critical: bottom.enable=false by default (ARCTERX/perturb.yaml:61) — off the default path; enabling it is the documented use for suspected strikes. Caveat: the specific ARCTERX numbers (6/12 false positives on files 0009/0026) are UNVERIFIABLE — those VMP .p files are absent from this checkout (only tests/data/SN479_0006.p present, which flagged None). Mechanism real and reachable when feature enabled → major.

confirmed_findings.title: Chi QC gate runs AFTER mixing-quantity derivation and never masks K_T/Gamma/K_rho: QC-rejected chi still drives finite published mixing coefficients
confirmed_findings.claim: In process_file's chi loop apply_qc_to_dataset runs after _add_mixing_quantities and its value_vars list omits K_T/Gamma/K_rho, so segments whose chi is QC-dropped (chiMean=NaN, drop_action='nan' being the documented default) keep finite K_T, Gamma, and K_rho computed from the rejected chi in the written chi NetCDF and hence in the binned/combo products — contradicting config.py:157-164's promise that drop_action='nan' NaNs the corresponding values so combos exclude them.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Call order: mk_chi_mean (pipeline.py:1490) -> _add_mixing_quantities computes K_T/Gamma/K_rho from pre-gate chiMean (:1495-1512) -> apply_qc_to_dataset with value_vars=['chiMean','chiLnSigma',chi_N...,'chi','epsilon_T'] (:1513-1529) -> to_netcdf (:1533). qc_gate.py:162-176 NaNs only listed vars. Reproduced twice independently: 3 QC-flagged segments end with chiMean=[nan nan nan] but K_T~1.98e-6, Gamma~0.81, K_rho~4.9e-7 all finite in the written NC; and an all-flagged dataset left K_T=1e-4, Gamma=0.2, K_rho=1e-4 unchanged. The epsilon side is ordered correctly (QC at :1388-1399 precedes the diss write). bin_by_depth then averages un-gated K_T/Gamma/K_rho into chi_binned/chi_combo while chiMean excludes the flagged segments. Latent on current ARCTERX yaml (qc drop lists empty) but fires on any configured use of the documented QC-gate feature.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: PYTHONPATH=/Users/pat/tpw/tpw/turbulence/src python3 scratchpad/repro_qc_mixing.py (replicates pipeline.py:1490-1533 exactly on a synthetic profile+chi dataset with a QC channel flagging segments 2-4; chiMean NaN but K_T/Gamma/K_rho finite) or scratchpad/verify_stats.py section 3. Cross-check ordering at pipeline.py:1490-1533 and the value_vars list at :1522-1527.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Move apply_qc_to_dataset before _add_mixing_quantities (mixing then consumes QC-NaN'd chiMean), or append K_T/Gamma/K_rho to the chi value_vars. Add a regression test that a QC-flagged segment has NaN in all chi-derived variables.
merged_from: Perturb QC gate runs after mixing-quantity derivation and never masks K_T/Gamma/K_rho, so QC-rejected chi still drives the published mixing coefficients

verdict.reasoning: Fully reproduced. Order confirmed: mk_chi_mean (pipeline.py:1490) → _add_mixing_quantities computes K_T/Gamma/K_rho from pre-gate chiMean (1495-1512) → apply_qc_to_dataset (1518) whose value_vars=['chiMean','chiLnSigma','chi_1','chi_2','chi','epsilon_T'] OMITS K_T/Gamma/K_rho → to_netcdf (1533). qc_gate.py:162-176 NaNs only listed vars. My repro (FakePF + exact value_vars): flagged segments 1-4 → chiMean=[nan,nan,nan,nan] but K_T=1.98e-6, Gamma=0.81, K_rho=4.9e-7 all finite. binning.py:250-272 bins EVERY 1-D numeric var of len n_depth, so K_T/Gamma/K_rho leak into chi_binned/chi_combo; no downstream recompute in combo.py/binning.py. NOT dup of #32 (that is the rsi path + fom/K_max_ratio QC; #32 explicitly treats perturb as correct). Off default path: qc.enable=True but drop_from lists empty + rules={} on ARCTERX yaml → latent; fires when the documented QC-gate is configured, producing wrong binned mixing numbers from rejected segments. Doc-promise wording (config.py:162-163 / yaml lists only chi_*/chiMean) is weaker than the finding states, but the core defect holds → major.

confirmed_findings.title: Interrupted binned.nc write leaves a partial file that validates as cache-current (manifest written before payload, no atomic write)
confirmed_findings.claim: A mid-write I/O failure (ENOSPC, SMB drop, Ctrl-C) during the binned.nc write leaves a readable binned.nc whose _input_manifest attribute already matches, so every subsequent perturb run logs 'Binning ... up to date (skipped)' and permanently publishes the truncated bins (and derived combos) with no error.
confirmed_findings.evidence: pipeline.py:117-119 (_write_binned_or_clear) does ds.assign_attrs(_input_manifest=manifest); ds.to_netcdf(out) — the cache-validity marker lands in the NetCDF header BEFORE the data payload completes, written direct to the final path (no tmp+os.replace, unlike trim.py:283-293 and _write_marker pipeline.py:333-335). Skip checks at :2252/:2273/:2290 compare only that attribute. Measured: simulated ENOSPC on the 2nd variable write left binned.nc (14282 B) with manifest_match=True, _output_is_current=True, epsilon 1000/1000 present but chi 0/1000 (all fill). Contrast the combo path which stamps AFTER a successful write (_stamp_manifest, :2400-2403).
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: PYTHONPATH=/Users/pat/tpw/tpw/turbulence/src python3 scratchpad/repro_binned_enospc.py — stage 1 injects OSError(ENOSPC) into netCDF4.Variable.__setitem__ on the 2nd write inside _write_binned_or_clear; stage 2 (fresh process) prints _output_is_current -> True while chi holds 0/1000 values.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Write binned.nc to a temp file and os.replace() it into place, and/or stamp _input_manifest only after to_netcdf returns (mirroring the combo path).

verdict.reasoning: Independently reproduced. _write_binned_or_clear (pipeline.py:117-119) does ds.assign_attrs(_input_manifest=manifest); ds.to_netcdf(out) — direct to final path, no tmp+os.replace. netCDF4/HDF5 writes global attrs + variable structure before filling data via Variable.__setitem__. My repro injected OSError(28,ENOSPC) on the 2nd data var: write raised, but left a readable 14144-B binned.nc. Fresh process: _read_manifest_attr='DEADBEEF_manifest_v1', _output_is_current=True, epsilon 1000/1000 finite, chi 0/1000 (all fill). So a later run logs 'up to date (skipped)' (skip checks pipeline.py:2252/2273/2290 compare only the attr) and publishes truncated bins; combo then rebuilds from the corrupt binned.nc. Contrast: trim.py:283-293 and _write_marker (pipeline.py:333-335) use tmp+os.replace; combo _stamp_manifest (2400-2403) stamps AFTER a successful write. Requires an I/O fault (ENOSPC/SMB drop/Ctrl-C) — realistic given the codebase's explicit exFAT/SMB/network-volume target (docstring 111-113, PR#86) — not the clean default path, so not critical; silent permanent corruption on re-run → major.

confirmed_findings.title: Bin/combo manifest is blind to per-file NC content and to per-file failure: a failed run's partial diss/chi NC is locked in by the successful retry
confirmed_findings.claim: When a per-profile to_netcdf aborts mid-write, the caught-and-continue handler leaves a readable partial NC on disk; that same run bins it into binned.nc stamped with a manifest built from the file's cache key, and the retry run — which reprocesses the file correctly into the same filenames — recomputes the identical manifest and skips re-binning, permanently publishing bins computed from the partial NC.
confirmed_findings.evidence: pipeline.py:1408 writes each diss NC inside try/except (:1412-1416) that records the error and continues without unlinking the partial file; the marker is correctly withheld (:1895) but file_cachekeys[stem] (:2100-2101) is set for EVERY file regardless of success and binning runs unconditionally over the glob (:2213), stamping binned.nc (:2272,:2286). _inputs_manifest (:127-142) hashes only [nc.name, cachekey] pairs — no content, no success bit — so the retry reproduces the manifest exactly and hits the skip at :2273. Measured: partial NC after injected ENOSPC read back epsilonMean 50/50 but chiMean 0/50; manifests before/after the complete rewrite compared equal; _output_is_current True.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: PYTHONPATH=/Users/pat/tpw/tpw/turbulence/src python3 scratchpad/repro_manifest_content_blind.py — aborts a diss-NC write after the first variable, stamps binned.nc with the run-1 manifest, rewrites the NC completely under the same name, shows _inputs_manifest unchanged and _output_is_current approving the stale binned.nc. Cross-check pipeline.py:1408/1412-1416, 2100-2101, 2272-2273.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Unlink out_path in the per-profile except handlers so a partial NC never survives, and/or poison the manifest for failed stems (fold a per-run nonce into file_cachekeys entries carrying errors).

verdict.reasoning: Mechanism confirmed by inspection + end-to-end repro. `_inputs_manifest` (pipeline.py:133-142) hashes only [nc.name, cachekey]; cachekey is a source-.p identity hash (2100-2101) set for EVERY file regardless of success; marker withheld on error (1895); binning runs unconditionally over the glob and stamps binned.nc (2213,2272,2286). Repro: run1 bins a partial (half-NaN) X_prof01.nc -> binned epsilonMean nanmean 2.11e-7; retry rewrites X complete (same name/cachekey) -> manifest byte-identical -> _output_is_current True -> re-bin skipped; correct re-bin would be 3.4e-7. So a stale bin IS retained. BUT reachability is failure-mode-conditioned: the common truncation outcome leaves an UNOPENABLE HDF5 (verified: all fractions raise NetCDF HDF error), and the scan/load workers (binning.py:426,458) have NO try/except, so run1 binning CRASHES LOUDLY (safe, no stamp). Only a soft ENOSPC during xarray's variable-write phase leaves an openable-partial NC (verified producible). Run1 also logs a visible error (1413). Impact bounded to the failed profile. Real default-path design gap in the post-#85/#86 cache, but conditioned on infra failure + openable-partial + no-force retry -> minor, not major. Finding's 'chiMean' evidence is spurious (diss NCs have no chiMean).

confirmed_findings.title: Trim cache fingerprints the source AFTER the trim copy: records appended during the trim are permanently dropped on later runs
confirmed_findings.claim: trim_p_file reads the source size, copies the trimmed output, and only then (_finish -> _trim_cache_store, trim.py:135) stats the source to record the cached fingerprint; any records appended between the size read (trim.py:242-243) and that stat (live acquisition writing the active .p, or an rsync completing mid-trim) match the marker on every later run, so _trim_cache_lookup (trim.py:116-126) serves the short trimmed file and the appended records are silently excluded from all epsilon/chi/CTD products until --force or another source change.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Verified end-to-end with a synthetic .p (128 B header, record_size 256): appending one complete 256 B record + 50 B partial during the trim window left dest at 960 B while the correct trim of the 4-record source is 1216 B; the next call WITH cache_dir returned action='skipped' dest_size=960 (stale, record 4 dropped), while the pre-#86 uncached path (cache_dir=None) correctly re-trimmed to 1216 B. The per-file processing cache gets the ordering right (cachekey fingerprinted BEFORE processing, pipeline.py:2100); only the PR #86 trim marker fingerprints after the work.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: PYTHONPATH=/Users/pat/tpw/tpw/turbulence/src python3 scratchpad/repro_trim_toctou.py — injects the concurrent append at tempfile.mkstemp time, then shows run-2-with-cache action=skipped dest_size=960 vs run-2-without-cache action=trimmed dest_size=1216.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Capture fp = _trim_fingerprint(source) at the top of trim_p_file (before opening the source) and pass that snapshot to _trim_cache_store.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed by inspection + repro. `_trim_cache_store` computes `_trim_fingerprint(source)` at trim.py:135 (a fresh stat) AFTER the source file_size used for the trim decision was read at 242-243 and after the copy (283-293). A write landing in that window makes the cached fingerprint describe a post-append state that was never trimmed to the stored dest. Repro (synthetic .p, record_size 256, first_record 192): concurrent append of one 256B record injected at mkstemp time -> run1 trims to dest_size 960 while source grows to 1316; run2 WITH cache (default: pipeline.py:1662 sets cache_dir) returns action=skipped dest_size=960 (record 4 permanently dropped); run2 WITHOUT cache correctly re-trims to 1216. Mechanism real, default path. But it is a TOCTOU race requiring (a) a concurrent append during the copy window AND (b) the source freezing at exactly the fingerprinted size for the next run (ongoing growth -> size mismatch -> cache miss -> correct re-trim). Common copy tools (rsync) use temp+atomic-rename, not in-place growth. Impact bounded to tail record(s) of one concurrently-written file. Edge-case race with bounded loss -> minor, not major.

confirmed_findings.title: ARCHITECTURE.md falsely claims rsi and perturb two-probe epsilon combines are 'mathematically identical' — measured 10x divergence on a disagreeing 2-probe window
confirmed_findings.claim: ARCHITECTURE.md lines 423-426 ('the inter-probe rejection is mathematically identical between the two — both either keep both probes or drop the larger one'), line 431 ('Same result for two probes'), and line 446 ('the two agree numerically for two-probe data') are false at HEAD: for the campaign's actual 2-probe VMP-250, perturb's mk_epsilon_mean NEVER drops a probe (CI loop gated on n_probes >= 3), while the rsi pipeline's ATOMIX flag path DOES drop the larger probe (bit 4), so the two published epsilon products diverge whenever the probes disagree beyond the 95% CI.
confirmed_findings.evidence: processing/epsilon_combine.py:135-136 gates the CI-removal loop on n_probes >= 3 (docstring: 'With only 2 disagreeing probes ... we KEEP BOTH'); scor160/l4.py:766-773 applies the bit-4 inter-probe flag for 2 probes and _compute_epsi_final (:798-800) geometric-means only flag==0 probes; rsi/pipeline.py:437 passes diss_length_s>0 so the flag is live on the default rsi-tpw path. Repro at HEAD (scratchpad/repro_two_probe.py): probes {1e-9,1e-7} W/kg, 0.7 m/s, 8 s window -> ln(e2/e1)=4.61 > CI 0.62; rsi flags=[0,4], epsilon_final=1e-9; perturb epsilonMean=1e-8 — 10x. The underlying 2-probe divergence was recorded as 06-25 audit M-finding (316x example) — this finding is the ARCHITECTURE.md text asserting the opposite at HEAD (describes pre-#79 behavior).
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: PYTHONPATH=/Users/pat/tpw/tpw/turbulence/src python3 scratchpad/repro_two_probe.py — rsi flags [0,4], epsilon_final 1e-09 vs perturb epsilonMean 1e-08. Read docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:420-448 vs epsilon_combine.py:135-136 and l4.py:766-773.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite the paragraphs: for 2 probes perturb intentionally keeps both while rsi's bit-4 flag drops the larger, so epsilon_final <= epsilonMean whenever the pair disagrees beyond 1.96*sqrt(2)*sigma_ln; delete the 'mathematically identical'/'agree numerically' sentences.

verdict.reasoning: Doc claim verified FALSE + 10x divergence reproduced. epsilon_combine.py:135-136 gates the CI-removal loop on n_probes>=3, so perturb ALWAYS keeps both probes for the 2-probe VMP-250 (docstring 131-134 'KEEP BOTH'). l4.py:766 applies bit-4 for epsi.shape[0]>1 and _compute_epsi_final (798) geomeans only flag==0; sigma_ln is live because diss_length_s>0 (l4.py:243; rsi/pipeline.py:437). Repro: probes {1e-9,1e-7}, ln-ratio 4.61 > threshold 0.62 -> rsi flags=[0,4], epsilon_final=1e-9; perturb epsilonMean=1e-8 -> 10x. So ARCHITECTURE.md:423-426/431/446 ('mathematically identical', 'Same result for two probes', 'agree numerically for two-probe data ... rather than science') is false at HEAD; the text describes pre-#79 'iterative max-removal'. Doc added by PR #69 (2026-06-17), predating the 06-25 audit, and NOT fixed by audit PRs #74-#81. The UNDERLYING 2-probe divergence duplicates 06-25 audit #121 (316x), but that finding cited epsilon_combine.py/l4.py, NOT ARCHITECTURE.md, so this specific misleading doc artifact is un-flagged and distinct. Severity minor (borderline major): real doc/code mismatch on publication-relevant behavior, but bounded — both products stay individually correct, only disagreeing 2-probe windows are affected, and the campaign publishes via one path.

confirmed_findings.title: Chi-vs-MATLAB spectra validation is statistically blind in the band chi is computed from: a deliberate factor-3 in-band spectral error still passes
confirmed_findings.claim: test_scalar_spectra_log_agreement (tests/test_matlab_chi.py:191-231 and clone tests/test_pipeline_vs_matlab.py:540-573) computes its median |log10(py/ml)| over ALL frequency bins including the 62% of bins above the f_AA=98 Hz anti-aliasing filter where the implementations legitimately diverge, so the 0.5-decade gate cannot detect even a factor-3 systematic error in the F<=f_AA band that chi is actually fitted from.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Measured on ARCTERX_Thompson_2025_SN479_0002 (20 matched profiles) at HEAD: full-range median|dlog10| = 0.306 (just under the 0.5 limit) but in-band (0<F<=98 Hz) 0.043; the 0.306 background is entirely above-AA junk (129 bins at df=2 Hz: 1-49 in-band, 50-128 out). Injection replicating the test's exact statistic: x2.0 in-band error moves the worst per-probe median 0.307->0.316 (PASSES); x3.0 -> 0.465 (PASSES); x0.5 -> 0.414 (PASSES). Additionally no test anywhere compares the chi VALUE to an independent reference: matlab/get_chi.m is a same-project port, not ODAS, and no *_chi validation NetCDF exists in VMP/.

confirmed_findings.title: Epsilon-vs-MATLAB magnitude gate is one full decade while actual agreement is 0.002-0.017 decades: a ~9.6x systematic epsilon error would pass the entire MATLAB validation
confirmed_findings.claim: The only assertions on epsilon magnitude in the MATLAB comparison suites are 'median |log10(py/ml)| < 1.0' (tests/test_pipeline_vs_matlab.py:421, tests/test_matlab_epsilon.py:218); every other epsilon check (correlation) is scale-invariant, so a systematic multiplicative epsilon error up to ~9.6x (wrong isotropy factor, dropped 2x normalization, wrong viscosity model) would pass the whole validation.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Measured at HEAD against MATLAB *_validation.nc (get_diss_odas): per probe-profile median |dlog10(eps)| for files 0002/0003/0004 = 0.007/0.004/0.002 (max single probe-profile 0.017) — the gate is 60-500x looser than demonstrated agreement. Since median|log10(f*py/ml)| ~ log10(f) +/- 0.017, any factor f < ~9.6 passes; correlation assertions at :425-453 use corrcoef of logs, invariant to constant factors.

Reproduced actual per-probe-profile agreement on files 0002/0003/0004: median|dlog10(eps)| = 0.034/0.019/0.011 (max 0.050) — 20-100x tighter than the 1.0 gate, so a multiplicative error up to factor ~9 passes this gate (log10(9)=0.95<1.0). confirmed_findings.title: QC-dropped and missing epsilon/chi bins are repainted with copied values by ffill_down in eps-chi and profiles figures
confirmed_findings.claim: Both pcolor kernels (layout.plot_panel used by perturb-plot eps-chi, layout.plot_columns used by perturb-plot profiles) forward-fill every internal NaN down each cast column AFTER QC masking, so bins that QC rejected (or that the pipeline already NaN'd, drop_action='nan' default) are rendered in publication figures with the value copied from the shallower bin — while the tool prints 'applied QC: NaN'd N bins' and titles the figure 'QC applied'.
confirmed_findings.evidence: eps_chi.py:375-390 NaNs eps/chi where qc_drop_*>0 then :435 calls layout.plot_panel whose first statement (layout.py:134) is z = ffill_down(z); profiles.py:261 NaNs QC cells then :271 calls plot_columns which ffills at layout.py:234. ffill_down (layout.py:57-76) fills ALL internal NaNs regardless of gap length. Verified end-to-end (_build_profiles_figure, apply_qc=True): cast eps [1e-9,2e-9,3e-8,4e-8,5e-8,6e-8,7e-9,8e-9] with bins 2-5 flagged prints 'QC flags NaN 4 cells per panel' yet the drawn QuadMesh contains [1e-9,2e-9,2e-9,2e-9,2e-9,2e-9,7e-9,8e-9] — 4/4 rejected cells rendered with fabricated values 15-30x smaller than the rejected data. Contradicts plot_columns' docstring (layout.py:229-230), --apply-qc docs (docs/perturb/plotting.md:219), and the set_bad 'visibly unsampled' convention (profiles.py:270). scalar subcommand is clean.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: PYTHONPATH=/Users/pat/tpw/tpw/turbulence/src python3 scratchpad/repro_ffill_qc.py and scratchpad/repro_profiles_e2e.py — both show 4/4 QC-dropped internal bins drawn with the shallower bin's value. Read eps_chi.py:375-390+435 and profiles.py:261+271 vs layout.py:134/:234.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Re-apply the QC/NaN mask after ffill_down (pass the pre-fill mask into plot_panel/plot_columns), bound the fill to <=1 bin or drop ffill entirely; update the plot_columns docstring.

verdict.reasoning: Mechanism reproduced: layout.ffill_down([1e-9,2e-9,nan,nan,nan,nan,7e-9,8e-9]) -> [...,2e-9,2e-9,2e-9,2e-9,...]; internal NaNs between valid bins are repainted with the shallower bin's value; leading/trailing stay NaN. Call chain confirmed: eps_chi.py:375-390 NaNs QC bins then :435 plot_panel -> layout.py:134 ffill_down(z); profiles.py:260-261 NaNs QC cells then :271 plot_columns -> layout.py:234 ffill_down before np.ma.masked_invalid. So --apply-qc-dropped INTERNAL bins render as continuous color under a 'QC applied' title, contradicting the --apply-qc contract and plot_columns' set_bad docstring (layout.py:229-230). Real but bounded: saved eps/chi NetCDFs are untouched; only internal QC drops sandwiched between valid bins are repainted (contiguous end drops stay masked), and the repaint is the nearest-neighbor value, not arbitrary garbage. This is a cosmetic/QC-fidelity doc-vs-code mismatch on publication pcolors, not wrong scientific numbers -> minor, not major. Finder's '15-30x smaller' figure is a synthetic contrivance (the rejected bin's true value is unknown by definition).

confirmed_findings.title: quick_look windowed eps/chi uses segment-length T grid with absolute indices: all-NaN epsilon/chi panels for every profile after the first (regression from PR #75 M-7 fix)
confirmed_findings.claim: _compute_windowed_eps_chi builds T_fast = _interp_slow_to_fast(T_slow, N) with N = the profile-segment length (quick_look.py:211,223) but slices it with absolute fast indices (:227-233), so for every profile with seg_start >= N the slice is empty, mean_T = NaN, nu = visc35(NaN) = NaN, and the viewer's epsilon and chi profile panels are entirely NaN; for the first profile the whole-record temperature is compressed onto the segment, silently biasing per-window viscosity.
confirmed_findings.evidence: quick_look.py:211 N = seg_end - seg_start; :223 T_fast = _interp_slow_to_fast(T_slow, N); :227-233 s = seg_start + idx*step; mean_T = mean(T_fast[w_sel]) while P uses full-length P_fast[w_sel] (:234). Correct pattern at viewer_base.py:285 uses len(P_fast). Reproduced end-to-end (repro_quicklook_T.py): profile slice(0,10000) -> 18/18 finite epsilon; slice(20000,30000) -> 0/18 finite; profile-1 window mean_T off -2.56 degC in a synthetic 25->10 degC record. git log -L shows the line introduced in commit 3b7e467 (PR #75, round-2 audit fixes), replacing mean(T_slow) — the M-7 fix itself created this regression, postdating the recorded audits.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: PYTHONPATH=/Users/pat/tpw/tpw/turbulence/src python3 scratchpad/repro_quicklook_T.py — T_fast[w].size==0 for a later profile, 0 finite epsilon for slice(20000,30000) vs 18 for slice(0,10000). Or open quick_look on any multi-profile ARCTERX .p and step to profile 2: 'No valid eps'.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Replace quick_look.py:223 with T_fast = _interp_slow_to_fast(T_slow, len(P_fast)) matching viewer_base:285; add a viewer regression test for a profile with sel.start > 0.

verdict.reasoning: Reproduced on real in-house data (tests/data/SN479_0006.p): quick_look._compute_windowed_eps_chi builds T_fast=_interp_slow_to_fast(T_slow, N) with N=seg_end-seg_start (quick_look.py:211,223) but slices it with absolute fast indices s=seg_start+idx*step (:227-233,:233). For the file's single profile seg_start=49632, N=103352: windows with s>=N get an EMPTY T_fast slice -> mean_T=NaN -> nu=visc35(NaN)=NaN -> NaN epsilon/chi. Measured finite eps=54/98 (deeper 22 windows x 2 probes silently NaN) and finite chi=54/98, while the correct grid (viewer_base.compute_windowed_diss uses _interp_slow_to_fast(T_slow, len(P_fast)), viewer_base.py:285) yields 98/98 on the identical profile. Shallow windows are also T-biased (whole record compressed onto the segment; synthetic 25->10C record: first-window mean_T off -2.3C). git log -L 223 confirms introduced in 3b7e467 (PR #75, the M-7 fix), postdating the recorded audits. seg_start>=N (finder's all-NaN-later-profiles case) is the multi-profile worst case. Interactive QA viewer, not saved-file numbers, but a silent, always-on loss of ~45% of the eps/chi profile panel plus viscosity bias on every real file -> major (silent data loss on realistic inputs).
verdict.failure_scenario: Open quick_look on any ARCTERX .p; epsilon/chi profile panels show the deeper windows as absent and shallower windows with T-biased viscosity.

confirmed_findings.title: Constant kappa_T = 1.4e-7 m^2/s ignores its temperature dependence: chi ~6.5% low and Method-2 epsilon_T ~12.6% low in ARCTERX 28 degC surface water, while viscosity in the same pipeline IS temperature-resolved
confirmed_findings.claim: Every chi (chi = 6*KAPPA_T*integral) and epsilon_T ((2*pi*kB)^4*nu*KAPPA_T^2) computation uses fixed KAPPA_T=1.4e-7 with no temperature dependence, which for ARCTERX Saipan (~28 degC surface, true kappa_T ~1.498e-7) biases chi low ~6.5% and Method-2 epsilon_T low ~12.6% (Method-1 kB high ~3.5%), inconsistent with the same pipeline computing per-window nu = visc(T,S,P).
confirmed_findings.evidence: batchelor.py:23 (KAPPA_T=1.4e-7); chi = 6*KAPPA_T*... at chi.py:263,456,534-538,579-585,612-619 and l4_chi.py:243-250; epsilon_T at chi.py:436,630; kB = batchelor_kB(epsilon,nu) default kappa at chi.py:229. Contrast l3_chi.py:177-197 per-window nu via visc(T,S,P). Computed kappa_T = k/(rho*cp) (Jamieson & Tudhope 1968/Sharqawy 2010 conductivity, gsw rho/cp, S=34.5): 1.389e-7 at 0 degC (-0.8%), 1.450e-7 at 15 (+3.6%), 1.498e-7 at 28 (+7.0%). chi linear in kappa_T (-6.5% at 28 degC), epsilon_T quadratic (-12.6%). docs/chi_mathematics.md:567 documents 1.4e-7 as 'Standard value' — a documented approximation but the single largest systematic chi bias on the warm-water default path, surfaced nowhere.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: Grep KAPPA_T under src/odas_tpw/chi/ (no call passes temperature-dependent kappa_T). PYTHONPATH=... python3 computing kappa_T(T)=k/(rho*cp) at S=34.5, p=10 for T=0/15/28; expect +7.0% at 28 degC; chi bias -6.5%, epsilon_T -12.6%.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Compute kappa_T per window from window-mean T (and S when available), threading it through batchelor_kB, the 6*kappa_T integrals, and epsilon_T recovery as nu already is; or document the +7%/-13% bias envelope in chi_mathematics.md Section 11.

verdict.reasoning: Mechanism and numbers confirmed. batchelor.py:23 KAPPA_T=1.4e-7 is used at every chi/epsilon_T site (grep of chi/: chi.py:263,265,436,456,536,570,581,608,614,630; l4_chi.py:245) with NO call passing a T-dependent kappa_T. Computed kappa_T=k/(rho*cp) (Jamieson&Tudhope/Sharqawy conductivity + gsw rho,cp, S=34.5, p=10): 1.389e-7 at 0C (-0.8%), 1.450e-7 at 15C (+3.6%), 1.498e-7 at 28C (+7.0%) — matching the finding exactly. chi is linear in KAPPA_T -> -6.5% at 28C; epsilon_T=(2pi*kB)^4*nu*KAPPA_T^2 quadratic -> -12.6% (kB fitted from shape, independent of kappa_T). Genuine internal inconsistency: same pipeline resolves nu=visc(T,S,P) (l3_chi) but freezes kappa_T. Medium confidence on severity: constant kappa_T~1.4e-7 is a widely-accepted, explicitly documented (chi_mathematics.md:567) community convention, and 6.5% is within microstructure uncertainty, so this is a bounded documented approximation rather than a defect; minor is the ceiling.

confirmed_findings.title: Windows entirely at/below the modeled noise floor return hard-coded floor values as finite chi data: the 1e-14 sentinel (_iterative_fit) and exactly 0.01*chi_vc grid-edge (_chi_from_epsilon)
confirmed_findings.claim: When every bin in the integration band satisfies Phi_obs <= Phi_noise, _iterative_fit returns the hard-coded sentinel 1e-14 K^2/s as a finite chi datum (kB=63245.6 cpm, epsilon_T=586.5 W/kg, fom=NaN), and _chi_from_epsilon returns exactly the grid-edge value 0.01*chi_vc with fom=0.79 that passes an upper-limit fom filter — both instead of NaN — and the rsi chi_final geometric mean ingests the sentinel while only the perturb path (chi_minimum=1e-13) masks it.
confirmed_findings.evidence: chi.py:540-541 and 599-600 set chi_obs=1e-14 when chi_band<=0; the final recompute (:607-626) leaves it; :636 returns it finite because kB_best is finite (grid ceiling), bypassing the kB=NaN guard added for the earlier audit fix. Measured (spec_obs=0.999*noise_K): chi=1e-14, kB=63245.553, epsilon_T=586.5, fom=NaN. Method 1: chi.py:268-270 chi_lo=0.01*chi_vc; for spec_obs=0.8*noise the log-LS cost is monotone so argmin is index 0 (no parabolic refinement at the edge, :289): measured chi=5.7628e-13 == 0.01*chi_vc exactly, fom=0.794. Downstream: _compute_chi_final (l4_chi.py:291-293) admits 1e-14 — probe pair (1e-9,1e-14) gives chi_final=3.16e-12, 316x below the good probe; chi_combine.py:100 masks chi<=1e-13 so perturb chiMean is protected from the sentinel only.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: PYTHONPATH=... python3: (a) _iterative_fit(0.999*noise_K, ...) -> chi==1e-14, kB==63245.553, epsilon==586.5, fom NaN; (b) _chi_from_epsilon(0.8*noise_K, K, 1e-9, ...) -> chi equals 0.01*(6*KAPPA_T*trapz(spec[valid])*correction) exactly; (c) _compute_chi_final(np.array([[1e-9],[1e-14]])) -> 3.16e-12.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Return NaN (not 1e-14) when chi_band<=0 in _iterative_fit, and NaN (or a flagged upper bound) when the Method-1 grid search terminates on a grid edge; both mean chi is below the detection limit.

verdict.reasoning: Reproduced both parts. (a) _iterative_fit on spec_obs=0.999*noise returns finite chi=1e-14 (sentinel from chi.py:600/636), kB=63245.553 (fine-grid ceiling 2*10^4.5, MLE pushes kB out on a rolloff-free noise spectrum), epsilon=488.8 W/kg (finding said 586; nonsense either way), fom=NaN. The kB=NaN guard (chi.py:636) is bypassed because kB is finite. _compute_chi_final (l4_chi.py:286-294) has no kB/fom/chi_minimum gate: geometric-meaning [1e-9,1e-14] gives 3.162e-12, 316x below the good probe. (b) _chi_from_epsilon(0.8*noise) pins chi to grid edge 0.01*chi_vc=1.4569e-15 exactly (no parabolic refine at index 0), fom=0.794. Impact bounded: perturb chiMean IS protected (chi_combine.py:100 masks chi<=1e-13, default), so only the secondary rsi chi_final is contaminated. Distinct from prior finding 2026-06-19:790 (that was kB=NaN leaking finite chi>1e-13 surviving perturb; here kB is finite-ceiling and the sub-1e-13 sentinel is caught by perturb).

confirmed_findings.title: compare_atomix.py falls back to HP_cut as the anti-aliasing frequency (truncating MSS Baltic integration at ~7.6 cpm, inflating the reported benchmark error 24x) and omits the ODAS 0.9 AA margin
confirmed_findings.claim: compare_atomix.py:282 resolves f_AA via attrs.get('f_AA', attrs.get('HP_cut', 98.0)), so for MSS_Baltic.nc (no f_AA attr, HP_cut=0.15) the harness uses a 0.15 Hz HIGH-PASS cutoff as the anti-aliasing limit, capping K_AA at the 7-cpm floor and misreporting Baltic agreement as log10 RMSD 0.192 instead of 0.008; additionally :400 omits the 0.9*f_AA margin that both ODAS and the production paths apply, so the harness never exercises the production epsilon configuration.
confirmed_findings.evidence: compare_atomix.py:282 and :400 (K_AA = f_AA / W). Verified on /Users/pat/tpw/ShearProbes_BenchmarkDescriptor/data/MSS_Baltic.nc: f_AA=None, HP_cut=0.15. As shipped: log10 bias +0.044, RMSD 0.192, corr 0.981, median K_max_rsi 7.6 cpm. With HP_cut fallback removed: bias +0.006, RMSD 0.008, corr 1.000, median K_max 20.8 cpm; median shipped/fixed eps ratio 1.070. Contradicts docs/atomix_benchmark.md:236-238 (MSS RMS 0.007-0.009). Production margin: get_diss_odas.m:372-373, rsi/dissipation.py:137, scor160/l4.py:213 (0.9*f_AA with NaN/floor guard the harness also omits). HP_cut conflates two physically unrelated filters (TidalChannel notes at :72 list both). The report text at :717 also misattributes the algorithm to rsi.dissipation while the import at :46 is odas_tpw.scor160.l4.

confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Never treat HP_cut as f_AA (use attrs.get('f_AA') with a non-constraining default and note it in the report); apply K_AA = 0.9*f_AA/W for parity; correct the report text to name scor160.l4._estimate_epsilon.

verdict.reasoning: Fully reproduced. MSS_Baltic.nc attrs: f_AA=None, HP_cut=0.15, so compare_atomix.py:282 f_AA=attrs.get('f_AA',attrs.get('HP_cut',98.0)) uses a 0.15 Hz high-pass as the anti-alias limit. Ran compare_dataset: SHIPPED bias +0.044, RMSD 0.192, corr 0.981, median K_max 7.6 cpm; dropping HP_cut (fallback to 98.0): bias +0.006, RMSD 0.008, corr 1.000, median K_max 20.8 cpm; median shipped/fixed eps ratio 1.070 — all matching the finding. Doc atomix_benchmark.md:230 reports MSS RMS 0.007-0.009 (matches FIXED, contradicts the shipped harness). Missing 0.9 margin confirmed: production l4.py:213 uses K_AA=0.9*f_AA/max(...) while harness :400 uses f_AA/W. HP_cut conflates a high-pass with an anti-alias filter. Report text :717 says 'rsi.dissipation' but import :46 is scor160.l4. Benchmark harness only, not production epsilon -> minor.

confirmed_findings.title: compare_atomix.py FOM plot compares the benchmark's Lueck MAD-based FOM against the package's variance-ratio fom — two different, empirically anti-correlated statistics — on a 1:1 axis with the 1.15 threshold
confirmed_findings.claim: plot_fom_comparison (compare_atomix.py:619-631) scatter-plots fom_atomix (Lueck 2022 MAD-based) against fom_rsi (observed/Nasmyth variance ratio) with a 1:1 line and 1.15 threshold lines on both axes, though these are different statistics; the harness never computes the comparable FM because _estimate_epsilon is called without num_ffts (:433-444).

The repo documents these as different (docs/epsilon_mathematics.md:513; rsi/dissipation.py:474-478 'NOT the MAD-based ATOMIX/Rockland FM statistic'); scor160/compare.py:352-356 correctly compares FM. Measured on VMP250_TidalChannel_024.nc: fom_atomix median 0.805 (0.250-1.250) vs fom_rsi median 1.010 (0.987-1.205), Pearson r = -0.53 — anti-correlated, so the 1:1/threshold comparison is meaningless.
