Read compare_atomix.py:433-444 (num_ffts not passed so FM is NaN). Compare scor160/compare.py:352-356.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Pass num_ffts (derivable from fft_length/diss_length attrs as scor160/cli.py:147 does) and plot the returned FM against fom_atomix; or retitle the axes naming the two statistics and drop the 1:1 line/threshold from the variance-ratio axis.

verdict.reasoning: Reproduced on VMP250_TidalChannel_024.nc: fom_atomix median 0.805 (range 0.250-1.250), fom_rsi median 1.010 (0.987-1.205), Pearson r=-0.530 — anti-correlated. plot_fom_comparison (compare_atomix.py:619-631) scatters these on a 1:1 line with 1.15 thresholds. They are different statistics: fom_rsi is the 5th _estimate_epsilon return = observed/Nasmyth variance ratio (l4.py:357; dissipation.py:474-478 explicitly 'NOT the MAD-based ATOMIX/Rockland FM statistic'); fom_atomix is the L4 Lueck-2022 MAD FOM. The harness never computes the comparable FM: the call at :437-444 omits num_ffts (default 0 -> FM NaN); SpectrumResult has no fm field. scor160/compare.py:352-356 does the correct pairing (computed.FM vs reference.fom). Benchmark harness visualization only -> minor. No prior audit covered compare_atomix (grep empty), so not a strict duplicate.

confirmed_findings.title: docs/epsilon_mathematics.md states the shear probe attenuates LOW wavenumbers — the Macoun-Lueck physics is backwards
confirmed_findings.claim: docs/epsilon_mathematics.md:264 says the airfoil probe's finite spatial extent 'attenuates the measured shear at low wavenumbers', but finite probe size spatially averages small scales and attenuates HIGH wavenumbers — the correction 1+(k/48)^2 in the same section grows with k, and the repo's own benchmark doc states the correct direction.
confirmed_findings.evidence: docs/epsilon_mathematics.md:264 vs the correction formula at :267-269 (amplification increasing with wavenumber, factor 2 at 48 cpm per :279) and docs/atomix_benchmark.md:139-141 ('averages the velocity field spatially, attenuating variance at high wavenumbers'). Macoun & Lueck (2004) model a spatial low-pass response. Code (l3.py:55-60) is correct; only the doc sentence is inverted.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: Read docs/epsilon_mathematics.md:264-279 and docs/atomix_benchmark.md:139-144 side by side; correction(48 cpm)=2, correction(0)=1. Cross-check Macoun & Lueck (2004), JTECH 21, 284-297.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Change to '...spatially averages the velocity field, attenuating the measured shear at high wavenumbers (small scales)'.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed by reading. epsilon_mathematics.md:264 states the airfoil probe's finite spatial extent 'attenuates the measured shear at low wavenumbers' — physically inverted. Macoun & Lueck (2004) model spatial averaging as a low-pass that attenuates HIGH wavenumbers (small scales). The correction in the same section, 1+(k/48)^2 (:267), grows with k (factor 2 at 48 cpm, :279) and is applied only below 150 cpm — an amplification that increases with wavenumber, i.e. it compensates for HIGH-wavenumber attenuation. The companion doc atomix_benchmark.md:139-141 correctly says 'attenuating variance at high wavenumbers.' Code is correct: l3.py:_apply_macoun_lueck computes 1+(kcyc/DENOM)^2 for kcyc<=MAX. Documentation-only single-word inversion; a careful reader has the correct formula alongside, so bounded impact -> minor.

confirmed_findings.title: Five code/metadata comments claim good FM 'approaches 0'; measured and theoretical good-fit FM is ~0.6-0.8, and near-zero FM would actually be anomalous
confirmed_findings.claim: The FM interpretation shipped in NetCDF variable attributes and QC comments ('Good fits approach 0; ATOMIX recommends rejecting FM > ~1.15' at rsi/dissipation.py:487-491, rsi/pipeline.py:751, rsi/chi_io.py:30,356,577) is statistically wrong: expected FM for a good spectrum is sqrt(2/pi)/T_M ~ 0.7-0.8, not ~0, so a user calibrating QC to 'FM near 0 = good' would misjudge normal estimates.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Theory: MAD of half-normal deviations has mean 0.798*sigma, so FM -> 0.798/T_M ~ 0.75-0.78 (T_M = 0.8+sqrt(1.56/N_s), dissipation.py:488-490). Measured: (a) end-to-end synthetic clean Nasmyth run (fs=512, nfft=1024, diss=4096, 299 windows) gives median FM 0.64-0.78 with 0-2.3% exceeding 1.15; (b) the ATOMIX VMP250 TidalChannel reference FOM on accepted-good data has median 0.805 (0.250-1.250). docs/epsilon_mathematics.md:517 states the correct interpretation, so the five comment sites contradict the package's own docs.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: grep -n 'approach 0' src/odas_tpw/rsi -> 5 sites. Run scratchpad/e2e_synth.py with PYTHONPATH set -> 'FM: median=0.644 p95=0.783 frac>1.15: 0.000'. Load benchmark L4 FOM from VMP250_TidalChannel_024.nc -> median ~0.8.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Reword all five comments: 'For statistically good fits FM is expected near 0.7-0.8 and below ~1 for 97.5% of spectra; ATOMIX recommends rejecting FM > ~1.15.'

verdict.reasoning: Mechanism real. l4.py:465 FM=mad_ln/(T_M*sigma_ln), sigma_ln=sqrt(1.25*N_eff^-7/9), T_M=0.8+sqrt(1.56/N_s). For a correctly-modeled (good) spectrum mad_ln=E[|N(0,sigma)|]=0.798*sigma, so FM->0.798/T_M in [0.74,0.86]. Ran MC (20000 draws): good-fit FM median=0.75, p5=0.54, p95=0.95, frac>1.15~0.002 — never near 0. So the comment 'Good fits approach 0' (dissipation.py:491, pipeline.py:751, chi_io.py:30,356,577) mis-states where good data sits, and epsilon_mathematics.md:517 gives the correct 'FM<1 for 97.5%'. But every site co-states the correct ATOMIX reject threshold (1.15), which is what actually drives QC, so no number, no QC decision, and no published product is affected — the harm is purely a conceptual misread of the statistic's target. Severity corrected minor->nit: it touches a QC-statistic's meaning (qualifies to report) but is scientifically inconsequential given the co-stated correct 1.15 cutoff.

confirmed_findings.title: docs/epsilon_mathematics.md says singular accelerometer bins retain the original spectrum; the code deliberately NaNs them
confirmed_findings.claim: docs/epsilon_mathematics.md:249 states 'If C_aa(f) is singular, the original spectrum is retained', but goodman.py NaNs singular-AA frequency bins on every path, with an inline comment explicitly rejecting the retain-raw behavior the doc describes.
confirmed_findings.evidence: docs/epsilon_mathematics.md:249 vs scor160/goodman.py:189-199 (comment: 'A singular AA[f] cannot be cleaned; NaN that bin ... rather than passing the UNCLEANED raw UU through'; clean_UU[f] = np.nan), :45-71 (_nan_singular_bins, cond>1e13), :263-265 (batch path). l4.py:373-386 then interpolates-or-rejects NaN bins.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: Read docs/epsilon_mathematics.md:245-259 and goodman.py:45-71,184-202,248-268. Optionally run clean_shear_spec with collinear accelerometer columns and confirm NaN bins.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Update step 2 of the doc: singular/rank-deficient (cond > 1e13) bins are set to NaN and excluded downstream; the uncleaned spectrum is never passed through as if cleaned.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed doc/code contradiction. epsilon_mathematics.md:249 states 'If C_aa(f) is singular, the original spectrum is retained.' The code does the opposite: goodman.py:189-199 fallback NaNs a singular AA[f] (comment: 'rather than passing the UNCLEANED raw UU through'), and _nan_singular_bins (goodman.py:45-71, cond>1e13) sets clean_UU[bad]=nan on every path (called unconditionally at :202). l4.py then interpolates-or-rejects the NaN bins. Retaining raw (as the doc says) would leave uncorrected vibration and bias epsilon high in that bin, so the doc describes the scientifically-wrong behavior. Impact is bounded: an exactly rank-deficient AA (duplicate/collinear accel channels) essentially never occurs on real 3-axis accelerometer data, so the behavioral divergence does not manifest on default in-house data — but a reader implementing from the doc would get the worse fallback. Genuine minor doc/code mismatch.

confirmed_findings.title: K_rho_max ceiling is one-sided QC: in the exact window class PR #81 masks, the algebraically identical K_T survives above 10 m^2/s, Gamma is unbounded (500 emitted), and the diagnosed-spurious epsilon still feeds the binned epsilon product
confirmed_findings.claim: mixing_coefficients masks only K_rho above K_rho_max=10 m^2/s (mixing.py:515-518), yet by the module's own identity (mixing.py:17-23, K_T == Gamma*eps/N2) the windows PR #81 masked as residual surface contamination publish a K_T of the same implausible magnitude whenever measured Gamma ~ 0.2, Gamma itself has no plausibility bound (500 emitted silently), and the epsilon that mixing.py:70-72 itself diagnoses as 'spurious' remains unmasked in epsilonMean/binned epsilon and in Gamma.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Ran mixing_coefficients at HEAD: (a) eps=1e-6, chi=1e-6, N2=1e-5, dTdz=1e-4 (all floors passed) -> K_T=50 m^2/s and Gamma=500 emitted with NO mask or warning, while any K_rho>10 is masked; (b) the PR #81-masked class eps=4e-3, N2=7e-5 with chi consistent with Gamma=0.2 -> K_rho=NaN (masked, warning) but K_T=11.43 m^2/s survives. mixing.py:70-72 says masked windows include 'extreme contaminated near-surface windows where epsilon is itself spurious', yet no flag propagates to epsilon/Gamma/K_T; git show 292ce35 confirms 14 such windows in the campaign run. rsi/binning.py:162-170 and perturb/binning.py then average the surviving spurious values into published bin means.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: PYTHONPATH=... python3 -c: mixing_coefficients([1e-6],[1e-6],[1e-5],[1e-4]) -> K_T=50, Gamma=500 unmasked. Then eps=4e-3, N2=7e-5, dTdz=0.05, chi=2*0.2*4e-3*0.05**2/7e-5 -> K_rho NaN + warning, K_T=11.43 finite. Read mixing.py:512-523 and :17-23; git show 292ce35.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Treat the ceiling as window-QC: mask K_T/Gamma at the same windows (or add K_T_max), consider flagging the window's epsilonMean contribution, or add per-window QC flag variables.

verdict.reasoning: Reproduced. mixing_coefficients (mixing.py:504-518) masks only K_rho>K_rho_max(10). Ran eps=1e-6,chi=1e-6,N2=1e-5,dTdz=1e-4 -> K_T=50 m^2/s and Gamma=500 emitted unmasked (both correct per formulas, no ceiling). Ran the PR#81-masked class eps=4e-3,N2=7e-5,dTdz=0.05,chi set to Gamma=0.2 -> K_rho=NaN (masked+warning) but the algebraically-identical K_T=11.43 survives (module even advertises the K_T==Gamma*eps/N2 identity at mixing.py:17-23). So the ceiling is one-sided: Gamma has no plausibility bound and a value of 500 (physically absurd; oceanic Gamma~0.2, rarely >2) is published silently for realistic low-eps stratified windows, and equally-large K_T survives. These are published NetCDF vars. Not critical/major: values are formula-correct derived diagnostics, easily filtered, and don't corrupt epsilon/chi. The adjacent 'spurious epsilon feeds binned epsilon' sub-claim is really an epsilon-QC matter (mixing never gates epsilon) and is weaker, but the one-sided-QC / unbounded-Gamma core is a real minor gap. Not covered by prior #30 (K_rho-only) or #32 (chi filtering).

confirmed_findings.title: K_rho NetCDF comment attributes in both pipelines and docs/mixing_efficiency.md still attribute the >10 m^2/s mask solely to 'near-floor N2', contradicting PR #81's own analysis and mixing.py's corrected comment
confirmed_findings.claim: Published-file metadata (rsi/pipeline.py:616-617 and perturb/pipeline.py:926-927: 'K_rho > 10 m2 s-1 (physically implausible diffusivity from near-floor N2)') and docs/mixing_efficiency.md:63-70 assert a single near-floor-N2 cause, although PR #81's commit message states the actually-masked windows are extreme near-surface epsilon windows with N2 ~ 7e-5 s^-2 and claims the messages were corrected — only mixing.py:70-72 and the runtime warning were.
confirmed_findings.evidence: grep 'near-floor N2' hits rsi/pipeline.py:617 and perturb/pipeline.py:927 (K_rho comment attrs written into published NetCDFs). git show 292ce35: 'the masked windows in this dataset are extreme near-surface eps, not near-floor N^2, so the message no longer asserts a single cause' — but the PR diff only changed '1' -> '10' in those attrs. docs/mixing_efficiency.md:69-70 says 'only the unbounded near-floor artifact is removed' while mixing.py:70-72 lists both causes.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: grep -n 'near-floor N2' src/odas_tpw/rsi/pipeline.py src/odas_tpw/perturb/pipeline.py; read docs/mixing_efficiency.md:62-70 vs mixing.py:66-73 and :519-522; git show 292ce353 diff.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Change both NetCDF comment attrs to mirror mixing.py ('unbounded near-floor-N2 artifact or contaminated near-surface epsilon'); fix mixing_efficiency.md accordingly.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed. git show 292ce35 (PR#81) on rsi/pipeline.py shows the K_rho comment attr diff is only '...K_rho > 1 m2 s-1' -> '...K_rho > 10 m2 s-1'; the cause clause 'physically implausible diffusivity from near-floor N2)' is an unchanged context line. Same single-cause text persists at rsi/pipeline.py:617 and perturb/pipeline.py:927, and docs/mixing_efficiency.md:68-69 ('only the unbounded near-floor artifact is removed'). Yet the same commit's message states the actually-masked 14 windows are 'extreme near-surface eps, not near-floor N^2 (N2~7e-5)', and it DID update the constant comment to two causes (mixing.py:71: 'the unbounded near-floor-N^2 artifact, OR extreme contaminated near-surface windows where epsilon is itself spurious'). So the published NetCDF metadata and the math doc name the wrong cause for this dataset, internally inconsistent with the code comment and the commit's own analysis. Bounded impact (mis-attributes why values are NaN'd; no numeric effect), but a real published-metadata error the author intended to fix everywhere and missed. Minor. Post-PR#81, not in the 2026-06-25/26 audits.

confirmed_findings.title: PFile.start_time omits the RSI recsize subtraction: every absolute timestamp in the pipeline is one record duration (+1.000 s) later than the ODAS reference
confirmed_findings.claim: PFile._read sets start_time directly from the record-0 header time words, but ODAS defines the data start time as that header time MINUS recsize (record duration, default 1 s), so all Python absolute time coordinates and cross-source merges are systematically +1 s late relative to Rockland's convention.
confirmed_findings.evidence: p_file.py:359-377 builds start_time with no recsize subtraction; odas/odas_p2mat.m:413-417 does t_start = addtodate(d.filetime, -recsize, 'second') where filetime is the same record-0 timestamp (read_odas.m:342) and setupstr.m:518-525 injects default recsize=1 (ARCTERX configs omit it; record span measured 1.0001 s). Measured on VMP/ARCTERX_Thompson_2025_SN479_0002.p: Python start_time 2025-01-14T15:30:17.445 vs MATLAB 15:30:16.445 (allch.nc attrs); python-minus-matlab = +1.00000437 s for every sample vs t_fast_YD. Propagates to L1 time coords (convert.py:454-462), per-profile time units/start_epoch_s (profile.py:353,365-367), and GPS/hotel/CTD merges (hotel.py:476, ctd.py:346) — ~1-2 m position bias at typical ship drift. No recsize handling anywhere in src/; no test compares absolute time to MATLAB.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: PYTHONPATH=... python3: load 0002.p PFile and the MATLAB _allch.nc; compare start_time+t_fast decimal-day against t_fast_YD -> +1.00000437 s all samples. Cross-check odas/odas_p2mat.m:413-417 and setupstr.m:512-525; confirm pf.config['root'] has no recsize.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: After parse_config, read recsize/recordDuration from config['root'] (default 1.0 per setupstr aliasing) and apply self.start_time -= timedelta(seconds=recsize); add a regression pinning start_time against the MATLAB _allch.nc time attrs.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed against ODAS. odas_p2mat.m:416-417 t_start=addtodate(filetime,-recsize,'second') where filetime is the record-0 header time (read_odas.m:342) and setupstr.m:523-525 injects default recsize=1 when both recsize/recordDuration are absent. Python p_file.py:359-377 builds start_time straight from the header time words with NO recsize subtraction; grep 'recsize' src/ finds only an unrelated config_patch note. Ran PFile on tests/data/SN479_0006.p: start_time=2025-01-16T01:57:58.863, t_fast[0]=0.0, and its config omits recsize -> ODAS would apply default 1 and label the first sample 01:57:57.863, i.e. Python is +1.000 s late (matches the finding's +1.00000437 s, the excess ~1/512 s being t_fast granularity). convert.py:454-461 propagates start_time into the L1 decimal-day time coordinate, so every absolute timestamp (and any absolute-time GPS/CTD/hotel merge) is +1 s vs the authoritative Rockland convention (~1 m at ship drift). Depth/pressure-indexed products (epsilon/chi/K/N2/Gamma) are unaffected, so minor rather than major. Distinct from prior #41 (tz-strip) and #95 (merge gap).

confirmed_findings.title: convert_accel adc_bits default changed to 16, diverging from ODAS's documented default of 0 — a PR #74 regression against an explicitly refuted audit claim
confirmed_findings.claim: convert_accel now defaults a missing adc_bits to 16.0 (scaling counts by 1/65536), but ODAS odas_accel_internal explicitly defaults adc_bits to 0 (2^0=1, counts-based coef0/coef1 calibration), so any legacy calibrated-accelerometer config that legitimately omits adc_bits produces acceleration wrong by up to two orders of magnitude vs the authoritative reference.
confirmed_findings.evidence: channels.py:195-196 _require_float(params,'adc_bits',16.0,'accel') vs odas/convert_odas.m:422-424 (catch -> adc_bits=0, adc_fs=1) — ODAS treats omission as a supported style. Introduced by commit 579ce0f (PR #74) even though docs/audits/2026-06-19-deep-audit-findings.json states 'The Python port's adc_bits default of 0 for accel faithfully matches ODAS and is NOT a divergence/defect' (PARTIALLY REFUTED verdict). Reproduced: counts [1000,-2000,15000] with {coef0:-100, coef1:1500} give Python [0.654,0.654,0.655] m/s^2 vs ODAS [7.19,-12.43,98.75] — ratios 11x-151x with sign flips. A warning is emitted (loud-but-wrong). Latent for ARCTERX (SN479 uses piezo).
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: PYTHONPATH=... python3: convert_accel(np.array([1000,-2000,15000],dtype=np.int16), {'coef0':'-100.0','coef1':'1500.0'}) vs 9.81*(d-(-100))/1500 -> ratios 11x-151x. Compare odas/convert_odas.m:418-428. git log -S on channels.py yields 579ce0f. Read the refutation in docs/audits/2026-06-19-deep-audit-findings.json.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Restore ODAS parity: adc_bits default '0', adc_fs default '1' for accel (keep warn-and-16 only for convert_voltage); add a test asserting the no-adc-params path reproduces 9.81*(counts-coef0)/coef1.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed. ODAS odas_accel_internal (convert_odas.m:424) 'try adc_bits=params.adc_bits; catch, adc_bits=0' -> a missing adc_bits means counts-based coef0/coef1 (2^0=1). Python channels.py:196 defaults 16 via _require_float (warns then returns 16). git log -S confirms PR#74 (579ce0f) flipped it from the ODAS-matching _safe_float(...,'0') to 16. The 2026-06-19 audit explicitly PARTIALLY REFUTED this exact accel case: 'ODAS odas_accel_internal ITSELF defaults adc_bits via catch=0 ... the Python port's adc_bits default of 0 for accel faithfully matches ODAS' — so PR#74 reintroduced a reference divergence. Reproduced: convert_accel(counts=[1000,-2000,15000],{coef0:-100,coef1:1500}) -> Python [0.654,0.654,0.655] vs ODAS 9.81*(d+100)/1500 = [7.19,-12.43,98.75]; ratios 11x/19x/151x with a sign flip. Bounded: latent for ARCTERX (SN479 uses piezo -> convert_piezo, not convert_accel), affects only legacy accel-type configs that omit adc_bits, and _require_float warns loudly ('physical units are suspect'). Real edge-case fidelity regression, minor. 2026-06-19 audit is not one of the two designated priors.

confirmed_findings.title: PFile never checks the per-record bad-buffer flag (header word 16) that ODAS mandatorily screens and repairs before conversion
confirmed_findings.claim: PFile._read stores all per-record headers but never inspects the bad-buffer flag (word 16, the field named buffer_status), whereas odas_p2mat unconditionally runs check_bad_buffers and patches flagged records before conversion — so a .p file with DAQ dropouts is silently converted with corrupted samples, feeding garbage into epsilon/chi.
confirmed_findings.evidence: p_file.py:426 is the only reference to record headers; grep for buffer_status/_record_headers returns only the _H definition (:45) and this assignment — no consumer. odas/odas_p2mat.m:347-365 (check_bad_buffers -> patch_odas -> fix_bad_buffers) and odas/check_bad_buffers.m:94 (bad_records = find(header(:,16) ~= 0)). Scanned all 29 ARCTERX files via _record_headers[:,15]: 0 flagged, so latent for this campaign, but any future deployment with a comm dropout is silently corrupted where ODAS detects and repairs.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: grep -rn 'buffer_status\|_record_headers' src --include='*.py' (only p_file.py:45,:426). Read odas/odas_p2mat.m:347-365 and check_bad_buffers.m:80-97. Rerun the scan: sum of count_nonzero(PFile(p)._record_headers[:,15]) over VMP/*.p -> 0.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: After reading records, warn on nonzero word-16 counts (and optionally NaN-out affected scan ranges); add a synthetic-.p test asserting the warning fires.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed. grep shows p_file.py:426 only stores `_record_headers = records['hdr']`; the sole other `buffer_status` reference is the _H dict at :45 — no consumer. ODAS `odas/odas_p2mat.m:348` unconditionally runs `check_bad_buffers` (which flags `header(:,16)~=0`, check_bad_buffers.m:94) and patches via patch_odas/fix_bad_buffers (:361,365) BEFORE `read_odas`. So PFile lacks the mandated bad-buffer screen. I scanned all 29 ARCTERX files via `_record_headers[:,15]`: 0 flagged (latent for this campaign). Real gap vs reference; a DAQ dropout would silently feed corrupt samples into eps/chi. Not a duplicate: prior 2026-06-25 #205 concerns merge.py chain-continuity (different file/purpose). Edge-case defect, bounded impact → minor.

confirmed_findings.title: compute_trim_depth's exit bin can lie below the deepest sample, so the caller applies NO trim to a never-settled cast — a bottom transient inside the search range on a shallow cast silently retains the surface prop wash
confirmed_findings.claim: When the deepest POPULATED bin in the search range is elevated (cast ends inside [min_depth, max_depth]), exit_idx = deepest+1 (top_trim.py:161) points at an unpopulated bin whose center exceeds every sample; the caller's 'above = np.where(P_seg >= trim_depth)' (perturb/pipeline.py:516-517) is then empty and the profile is left completely untrimmed, retaining the surface wash the stage exists to remove.
confirmed_findings.evidence: Synthetic repro (verified): cast 0.2-28.0 m, wash at 0-4 m plus a strong transient >27.6 m, defaults: compute_trim_depth returns 28.5 m > cast max 28.0; caller above-count = 0 -> no trim, wash kept. The min(deepest+1, len-1) clamp at :161 guards only the array bound — the same 'reported depth beyond deepest sample' geometry class as prior-audit M4 in bottom.py, which was fixed there (bottom.py:143-153) but not applied to top_trim. Not triggered in the 29 in-house files (min cast 74.9 dbar > max_depth).
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: PYTHONPATH=... python3: depth=linspace(0.2,28.0,20000), ax/ay noise x500 for depth<4 and depth>27.6; compute_trim_depth returns 28.5 and (depth>=28.5).sum()==0.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Clamp the returned trim depth to the deepest observed sample in the search range (or report the exit bin's sample mean as bottom.py now does); caller should treat empty 'above' with a not-None trim depth as 'never settled' and drop or flag the profile.

verdict.reasoning: Reproduced with DEFAULT config (max_depth=50): cast 0.2-28.0 m, surface wash <4 m + transient >27.6 m → compute_trim_depth returns 28.5 m (>cast max 28.0), so caller `np.where(P_seg>=28.5)` (pipeline.py:516) is empty → NO trim, wash retained. Mechanism: deepest elevated bin is last populated bin; exit_idx=deepest+1 (top_trim.py:161) is an unpopulated bin center below every sample; the min(...,len-1) clamp only guards the array bound. Same geometry class as prior M4 but in a DIFFERENT file (top_trim.py vs bottom.py where it was fixed) → not a duplicate. Reachability: top_trim disabled by default; ARCTERX enables it with max_depth=25 but all casts >74.9 dbar, so unreachable on in-house data (needs a shallow cast ending inside search range). Edge-case latent bug → minor.

confirmed_findings.title: top_trim docstring claims the median combine is 'robust to a single misbehaving channel', but the production caller feeds exactly two channels (Ax, Ay) where median = mean, and one-channel abstention leaves a single channel deciding the trim
confirmed_findings.claim: The docstring (top_trim.py:66-67) and comment (:165-167) promise median robustness to one misbehaving channel, but with the VMP caller's fixed two-channel feed (perturb/pipeline.py:509, Ax/Ay only) np.median of two exits is their MEAN — one bad channel drags the trim halfway — and the abstention rule (:152-156) means a single barely-elevated channel alone sets the trim whenever the other sees no wash.
confirmed_findings.evidence: pipeline.py:509 iterates ('Ax','Ay') only; tests/test_processing_top_trim.py:79-105 exercises robustness with THREE channels, unreachable in production. Real-data: 0025 prof 2 — Ax abstains, Ay's elevated bins only 2.0-2.6x background, Ay alone sets trim=9.0 m vs a 3.0 m contiguous-run exit; 0026 prof 5 — Ay alone trims to 8.5 m; 0028 prof 14 — Ax exit 2.0 m, Ay 7.5 m -> trim 4.75 m (averages rather than rejecting). Only the flat/dead-channel special case (:127-132) matches the docstring.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: Read top_trim.py:66-67,:164-168 vs perturb/pipeline.py:508-511. Run the survey on 0025 profile 2 with per-channel diagnostics. Note np.median([a,b]) == (a+b)/2.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Reword the docstring to state the two-channel reality, or make the combine robust for n=2 (min exit on large disagreement, or require both channels to confirm bins deeper than the contiguous surface run).

verdict.reasoning: Doc/context mismatch, verified by inspection. Production caller pipeline.py:509 feeds only ('Ax','Ay') — exactly two channels — and np.median([a,b])==(a+b)/2 (mathematical fact), so the median offers NO outlier rejection for a misbehaving-but-live channel; it averages the two. The docstring (top_trim.py:66-67) and comment (:164-168) claim the median is 'robust to a single misbehaving channel', which only holds for N>=3 (tests use 3 channels). The dead-channel clause IS satisfied even at N=2 (flat channel dropped, :127-132; abstention :152-156). So the robustness claim overstates for the actual 2-channel feed; affects trim depth (near-surface data retention). Genuine but low-value doc imprecision → minor. Kept low confidence: the docstring describes a general N-channel function and median-of-2 still beats the `max` alternative it contrasts.

confirmed_findings.title: perturb profile detection smooths fall rate with the fixed default tau=1.5 instead of the vehicle-resolved tau, inconsistent with its own speed computation and with ODAS
confirmed_findings.claim: In _process_file, profile detection uses W = _smooth_fall_rate(P_slow, pf.fs_slow) with hardcoded default tau=1.5 (pipeline.py:1140) while the same function resolves profile DIRECTION per-vehicle (:1146-1151) and the speed channel uses resolve_tau(vehicle) (rsi/speed.py:80, e.g. 3.0 slocum_glider, 60.0 argo_float); ODAS smooths the detection fall rate with the vehicle tau (odas_p2mat.m:696-700), so non-VMP vehicles get a 2-40x too-high smoothing cutoff, making W noisier at the W_min threshold and profile boundaries more fragmented.
confirmed_findings.evidence: pipeline.py:1140 (no tau argument; scor160/profile.py:33 default tau=1.5) vs rsi/speed.py:80 and rsi/vehicle.py:16. Reference: odas/odas_p2mat.m:696-700 (butter(1,(0.68/tau)/(fs_slow/2)) with vehicle tau). Same gap in rsi/profile.py:146 (extract_profiles exposes no tau kwarg; the _VEHICLE_TAU dict at rsi/profile.py:36-42 is dead code). Zero effect on ARCTERX VMP (tau=1.5 is the vmp default); impact confined to glider/float/AUV inputs which the project does process. Prior audit round-2 M-5 covered only the analogous rsi/pipeline.py L2Params bug, not this path.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: Read perturb/pipeline.py:1140 vs :1146-1151 and rsi/speed.py:80; odas/odas_p2mat.m:696-700. grep -rn _VEHICLE_TAU src/ (no consumers).
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Pass tau=resolve_tau(vehicle) (or reuse the vehicle-tau-smoothed W_slow channel injected at :1089) into _smooth_fall_rate; add a tau/vehicle kwarg to extract_profiles or delete the dead _VEHICLE_TAU alias.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed self-inconsistency. pipeline.py:1140 `W = _smooth_fall_rate(P_slow, pf.fs_slow)` passes no tau → default 1.5 (scor160/profile.py:33), while direction IS vehicle-resolved 6 lines later (:1146-1151), the speed channel uses resolve_tau(vehicle) (speed.py:80), and ODAS smooths detection W_slow with vehicle tau (odas_p2mat.m:696: `tau=d.vehicle_info.tau`, butter(1,(0.68/tau)/(fs/2))). VEHICLE_ATTRIBUTES gives slocum_glider 3.0, argo_float 60.0, auv 10.0 → cutoff 0.68/tau is 2-40x too high (less smoothing) for non-VMP. Same gap at rsi/profile.py:146; `_VEHICLE_TAU` (rsi/profile.py:42) confirmed dead (grep: only the assignment). Zero effect on ARCTERX VMP (tau=1.5 default); impact confined to glider/float/AUV inputs the vehicle machinery is built to support. Not a prior duplicate. Latent inconsistency → minor.
verdict.duplicate_of_prior_reasoning: prior smooth_fall_rate findings were NaN/fill, not tau

confirmed_findings.title: Stale combo.nc (and binned.nc) republished when a re-run's input set yields no binned data
confirmed_findings.claim: The #56 fix clears a stale binned.nc when a re-run produces an empty binned dataset, but the combo stage has no equivalent: make_combo returns None for the now-empty binned dir and _run_combo leaves the previous run's combo.nc as the apparently-current product; if the input set shrinks to zero per-profile NCs, binning is skipped entirely and BOTH stale binned.nc and combo.nc persist.
confirmed_findings.evidence: pipeline.py:99-121 _write_binned_or_clear unlinks binned.nc on empty ds; _run_combo (:2386-2406) only writes/stamps when make_combo returns non-None (:2400-2404) and never deletes dst/'combo.nc' (combo.py:75-77 returns None on no inputs). Zero-input variant: :2202/:2250. Reproduced: run 1 wrote combo.nc (2 profiles); run 2 with empty dataset removed binned.nc but combo/combo.nc still exists with run-1 data. Dirs are config-hashed only, so reused across input sets.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: Repro inline with PYTHONPATH set: _write_binned_or_clear(real ds) + _run_combo -> combo.nc exists; _write_binned_or_clear(xr.Dataset()) unlinks binned.nc; _run_combo again -> combo.nc still present with run-1 contents. Or read pipeline.py:2386-2406 and combo.py:71-77.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: When src exists but yields no input NCs, unlink dst/'combo.nc'; also clear binned.nc/combo.nc on the zero-input branch at :2202/:2250.

verdict.reasoning: Reproduced. Ran _write_binned_or_clear(real 2-profile ds)→binned.nc + make_combo→combo.nc(2 profiles); then _write_binned_or_clear(empty xr.Dataset())→binned.nc UNLINKED (pipeline.py:120-121, the #56 fix) but make_combo returns None (combo.py:76-77 empty glob) and combo.nc STILL holds run-1 data (2 profiles, epsilonMean present). _run_combo (pipeline.py:2398-2406) only stamps/logs when out is not None and never deletes dst/'combo.nc'; _output_is_current(combo.nc, None)=False (:148) so it rebuilds but the rebuild yields None. Output dirs are config-hashed only (not input-set), so the same dir is reused across shrinking input sets — the exact scenario the #56 binned fix targets, left unhandled for the combo (the final published product). Edge-case stale-cache → minor.

confirmed_findings.title: Perturb combo 'bin' coordinate holds pressure (dbar) but is labeled depth in metres (units='m', standard_name='depth') — contradicting the rsi L5 labeling of the same computation and splitting the plot modules
confirmed_findings.claim: The perturb pipeline depth-bins profiles/diss/chi on pressure variables (P/P_mean, dbar) but the published combo NetCDFs stamp the resulting 'bin' coordinate with units='m', standard_name='depth' plus geospatial_vertical_units='m'/EPSG:5831, while the rsi pipeline labels the identically-computed coordinate 'dbar'/'sea_water_pressure' — a systematic ~0.7-1% mislabel (500 dbar = 496.48 m) in the published CF coordinate, and the plot modules contradict each other on the same axis (eps_chi.py:439/scalar.py:223 'Depth (m)' vs profiles.py:267 'Pressure (dbar)').
confirmed_findings.evidence: binning.py:193 _DEPTH_CANDIDATES=('depth','P','P_mean') and :226-231 pick the first present; rsi/profile.py writes P but never depth; diss/chi carry only P_mean (dissipation.py:534-540 units 'dbar'). netcdf_schema.py:44-51 COMBO_SCHEMA['bin'] units 'm', standard_name 'depth' (CHI_SCHEMA reuses at :347); combo.py:105 stamps it and :193-205 advertises geospatial_vertical_units 'm' + EPSG:5831 from dbar values. rsi/binning.py:183-190 correctly labels depth_bin units='dbar', standard_name='sea_water_pressure'. profiles.py:18-21 module docstring even calls the bin:units='m' attribute 'a known mislabel'; eps_chi.py:106 comments the bin grid is 'pressure' yet :439 labels the axis 'Depth (m)'. Verified: synthetic per-profile NC with P_mean 5-500 dbar -> bin centers 5.5..499.5 -> apply_schema attrs {'units':'m','standard_name':'depth'}. Magnitude (gsw, lat 15N): 100 dbar=99.39 m, 500=496.48, 1000=991.77 — up to ~4 m at 500 m for any CF consumer.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: 1) grep _DEPTH_CANDIDATES binning.py:193; confirm no 'depth' var in profile/diss/chi writers. 2) PYTHONPATH=... python3: build Dataset with P_mean=linspace(5,500,50)+epsilonMean, bin_by_depth then apply_schema(COMBO_SCHEMA); print bin values/attrs. 3) Compare rsi/binning.py:183-190. 4) Compare profiles.py:267 vs eps_chi.py:439/scalar.py:223,256. 5) python3 -c 'import gsw; print(-gsw.z_from_p(500,15))' -> 496.48.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Either relabel COMBO_SCHEMA/CHI_SCHEMA 'bin' as units='dbar', standard_name='sea_water_pressure' (matching rsi) and fix combo.py geospatial attrs plus eps_chi.py/scalar.py axis labels to 'Pressure (dbar)'; or convert P to true depth via gsw.z_from_p(P, lat) before binning (matching the CTD product). Add a test asserting bin coordinate units agree with the binned source variable.
merged_from: Depth-binned combos bin by pressure (dbar) but publish the coordinate as depth in metres (standard_name=depth, units=m)
eps-chi y-axis labeled 'Depth (m)' but plots the pressure (dbar) bin coordinate

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed on REAL output. Inspected ARCTERX/.../combo/combo.nc: `bin` coord values 1.5..879.5 with attrs units='m', standard_name='depth' (from COMBO_SCHEMA netcdf_schema.py:44-51), plus global geospatial_vertical_units='m', EPSG:5831, vmin/vmax=1.5/879.5. But those values are PRESSURE: binning._DEPTH_CANDIDATES=('depth','P','P_mean') (:193) and per-profile NC has no 'depth' var — P is units='dbar'/standard_name='sea_water_pressure' (verified). So published CF vertical coord mislabels dbar as depth-m (~0.7-1%, e.g. 500 dbar=496.48 m, 880 dbar≈872 m). rsi/binning.py:183-190 correctly labels depth_bin 'dbar'/'sea_water_pressure'. Self-acknowledged: profiles.py:18-21 docstring calls bin:units='m' 'a known mislabel' and profiles.py:267 axis 'Pressure (dbar)', yet eps_chi.py:439 & scalar.py:223 label 'Depth (m)' (eps_chi.py:106 comment even says 'pressure'). Real systematic mislabel + internal inconsistency; dissipation values correct and impact bounded by the standard dbar≈m convention → minor.

confirmed_findings.title: CHI_SCHEMA omits the headline chiMean/chiLnSigma (published with no units/long_name) while 8+ schema entries reference per-probe variables (FM_1/2, K_max_1/2, kB_1/2, fom_T_1/2, K_max_T_1/2) that never exist in any combo
confirmed_findings.claim: The chi combo's primary variables chiMean and chiLnSigma have no CHI_SCHEMA entry, so after binning strips all variable attrs they are published without units or long_name; meanwhile CHI_SCHEMA's kB_1/kB_2, fom_T_1/fom_T_2, K_max_T_1/K_max_T_2 and COMBO_SCHEMA's FM_1/FM_2, K_max_1/K_max_2 describe per-probe variables no code ever creates — the underlying fom/FM/K_max/kB are 2-D (probe,time) and silently dropped by the binning loader, so the published combos carry no per-probe QC diagnostics at all.
confirmed_findings.evidence: chi_combine.py:193-211 creates chiMean/chiLnSigma; binning.py _load_profile_snapshot reads values only (:249-272) and skips non-1-D vars (:254, 'if len(shape) != 1 ... continue'); apply_schema (netcdf_schema.py:448-474) is the sole attrs source and CHI_SCHEMA (:296-367) lacks chiMean/chiLnSigma. Schema dead entries at :238-259 and :307-333 plus _COVERAGE_CONTENT_TYPE:415-422; module header cites 'Reference: Code/Combo.json'. Reproduced two ways: (a) synthetic chi binned.nc + make_combo(CHI_SCHEMA): chiMean units=None, long_name=None; qc_drop_chi flag_meanings lost; (b) synthetic diss NC with 1-D e_1/e_2/epsilonMean plus 2-D fom/FM/K_max binned -> data_vars == ['e_1','e_2','epsilonMean'], FM_1 absent. grep: no module creates FM_1/kB_1/fom_T_1/K_max_T_1 (only e_N/chi_N promoted, epsilon_combine.py:60-74, chi_combine.py:80-92); git log -S FM_1 shows the name born in the schema file only. Adjacent to prior finding n35 (empty-string units on LISTED diagnostics) but materially different: MISSING entries on headline variables plus dead entries.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: 1) Build a binned.nc with chiMean/chiLnSigma, make_combo(dir,out,CHI_SCHEMA,method='depth'), print attrs -> units=None. 2) grep -rn 'fom_T_1\|FM_1\|kB_1\|K_max_T_1' src/odas_tpw --include='*.py' | grep -v netcdf_schema -> empty. 3) Confirm the 1-D skip at binning.py:254. 4) git log --all -S 'FM_1' -- src.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Add chiMean (K^2/s), chiLnSigma ('1'), and qc_drop_* flag entries to CHI_SCHEMA; either delete the dead per-probe entries or flatten per-probe QC to 1-D companions (FM_1=FM[0], ...) in mk_*_mean so they survive into combos. Extend test_perturb_netcdf_schema.py to assert every combo data_var has units.
merged_from: COMBO/CHI schemas declare per-probe QC variables (FM_1/2, K_max_1/2, fom_T_1/2, K_max_T_1/2, kB_1/2) that no code ever creates — per-probe QC silently never reaches the published combos

verdict.reasoning: CONFIRMED. Reproduced end-to-end: built binned chi NC with chiMean/chiLnSigma, ran make_combo(CHI_SCHEMA) -> chiMean units=None, long_name=None (chi_1 correctly units='K^2/s'). netcdf_schema.py:296-367 CHI_SCHEMA has no chiMean/chiLnSigma; chi_combine.py:193-208 sets units on the per-profile file but binning.py:503-505 writes 2-D binned vars without attrs, and combo.py:139 apply_schema is the sole attrs source. Primary claim (headline chiMean/chiLnSigma published without units) is NOT in the two named audits (06-26 #8 is general no-units, addressed by apply_schema; 06-25 n35 is empty-string units on LISTED diagnostics). Dead-entries part overlaps the un-scoped 06-19 audit only. Metadata defect, no wrong numbers -> minor.

confirmed_findings.title: epsilon.diagnostics / chi.diagnostics / profiles.diagnostics are documented no-ops: the promised diagnostic variables are never written
confirmed_findings.claim: The config template and docs promise that profiles.diagnostics writes 'T1_raw, C_raw, W, cal attrs', epsilon.diagnostics writes 'spec_shear_raw, spike_count, probe_mask', and chi.diagnostics writes 'spec_noise, fp07_transfer, gradT_raw', but process_file strips 'diagnostics' from the epsilon/chi kwargs, never reads profiles.diagnostics, and the compute functions have no diagnostics parameter — setting these keys silently does nothing (unlike ctd.diagnostics and binning.diagnostics, which are honored).
confirmed_findings.evidence: pipeline.py:1370-1377 excludes 'diagnostics' from _compute_epsilon kwargs; :1427-1433 from chi kwargs; grep shows only ctd (:1241) and binning (:2176) consumers; zero matches in rsi/dissipation.py, rsi/chi_io.py, rsi/profile.py. Claims: config.py template :556, :607, :635; docs/perturb/configuration.md:81,167,191.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: grep -n diagnostics src/odas_tpw/perturb/pipeline.py src/odas_tpw/rsi/{dissipation,chi_io,profile}.py; confirm _compute_epsilon (dissipation.py:73-84) and _compute_chi (chi_io.py:36-45) have no diagnostics parameter. Compare config.py:556,607,635 and configuration.md:81,167,191.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Implement the promised outputs or mark the flags reserved/unimplemented in template and docs (as done for the unused bottom.* keys).

verdict.reasoning: CONFIRMED. pipeline.py:1376 strips 'diagnostics' from eps_cfg kwargs; :1431 strips it from chi_cfg. grep 'diagnostics' in rsi/dissipation.py, rsi/chi_io.py, rsi/profile.py returns EMPTY (no such parameter); profiles.diagnostics is never read. Only ctd.diagnostics (pipeline.py:1241) and binning.diagnostics (:2176) are honored. Yet config.py:556 comments 'write T1_raw, C_raw, W, cal attrs', :607 'write spec_shear_raw, spike_count, probe_mask', :635 'write spec_noise, fp07_transfer, gradT_raw', and configuration.md:81/167/191 promise 'Include diagnostic variables.' Setting these keys silently does nothing. Real config-comment/doc vs code mismatch, no wrong numbers -> minor. Not in named audits (06-26 #86 is an unrelated diagnostics-hash issue).

confirmed_findings.title: Chi Method 2 (use_epsilon=false, chosen because shear epsilon is distrusted) still builds published Gamma and K_rho from that same shear epsilonMean
confirmed_findings.claim: When chi.use_epsilon=false — documented as the setting for instruments whose shear-probe epsilon is contaminated — _add_mixing_quantities nonetheless opens the diss product and pairs its shear epsilonMean into Gamma = N2*chi/(2*eps*(dT/dz)^2) and K_rho = 0.2*eps/N2, so the published mixing coefficients are driven by the very epsilon the configuration declared unreliable, with no attribute or warning distinguishing this from Method 1.
confirmed_findings.evidence: pipeline.py:1461 sets diss_ds=None for Method 2, but :1495-1500 re-opens diss_path for mixing; the in-code Method-2 rationale (:1457-1460 'shear epsilon is contaminated') and config.py:617-621 contradict feeding it into Gamma/K_rho. The chi dataset already carries epsilon_T (chi-derived), the internally-consistent choice. Variable comments at :908-911/:921-927 say only 'epsilonMean paired from the nearest dissipation window'.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: Read pipeline.py:1453-1512: with chi_use_epsilon False, mixing still opens diss_path (:1499) and passes epsilonMean to pair_nearest/mixing_coefficients (:882-887). Confirm rationale at :1457-1460 and config.py:617-621.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: For use_epsilon=false, derive mixing epsilon from the chi product's epsilon_T, or record the shear-epsilon provenance in the K_rho/Gamma comment attrs and warn; make it configurable (chi.mixing_epsilon: shear|epsilon_T).

verdict.reasoning: Mechanism CONFIRMED. pipeline.py:1461 sets diss_ds=None for Method 2 (use_epsilon=false), but :1496-1500 re-opens diss_path as mix_eps for mixing; :1502 passes it to _add_mixing_quantities, where :882-887 pairs diss_ds['epsilonMean'] (shear) into mixing_coefficients. mixing.py:507 Gamma=N2*chi/(2*eps*dTdz^2) and :510 K_rho=0.2*eps/N2 both use that shear epsilon. config.py:619-621 explicitly states use_epsilon=false is 'for instruments where shear epsilon is unreliable,' so the mixing coefficients are built from the epsilon declared unreliable, with no distinguishing attribute (var comments 908-927 say only 'paired from the nearest dissipation window'). Genuine self-inconsistency. BUT: not the default path (ARCTERX inherits use_epsilon=true, config.py:122); and K_rho-from-shear-epsilon (Osborn) is standard while chi-side epsilon_T is not obviously better, so the 'wrong' framing is debatable. Bounded, non-default, edge-case -> minor, not major.

confirmed_findings.title: ARCTERX campaign config comments misstate the default dissipation window: '2 * fft_length' (epsilon) and '3 * fft_length' (chi) vs the actual 4 * fft_length
confirmed_findings.claim: The checked-in campaign config used for the publication dataset annotates 'diss_length: null' as '= 2 * fft_length (default)' for epsilon (line 76) and '= 3 * fft_length (default)' for chi (line 93), but every default path uses 4 * fft_length — a reader infers a 0.5 s epsilon window when the pipeline averages over 2 s (1024 samples at 512 Hz), misstating vertical resolution, num_ffts (3 or 5 inferred vs actual 7), spectral dof (dof_spec = 1.9*(num_ffts-n_v)), the FM statistic's N_f, and the averaging length L in the Lueck variance model.
confirmed_findings.evidence: ARCTERX/perturb.yaml:76 and :93 vs rsi/dissipation.py:101-102 ('diss_length = 4 * fft_length'), rsi/chi_io.py:68-69 (same), perturb/pipeline.py:1343-1347 ('or 4 * fft_length'), and the template/docs which correctly say 'null = 4 * fft_length' (config.py:589,612; docs/perturb/configuration.md).
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: sed -n '70,96p' ARCTERX/perturb.yaml; grep -n 'diss_length = 4' src/odas_tpw/rsi/dissipation.py src/odas_tpw/rsi/chi_io.py; grep -n '4 \* eps_cfg\|4 \* chi_cfg' src/odas_tpw/perturb/pipeline.py.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Correct both YAML comments to '= 4 * fft_length (default)' (or set explicit values if 2x/3x windows were actually intended — a processing-parameter decision to revisit).
merged_from: ARCTERX production config comments claim diss_length defaults of 2x (epsilon) and 3x (chi) fft_length; the code default is 4x for both, misstating window length, segment count, and dof

verdict.reasoning: CONFIRMED. ARCTERX/perturb.yaml:76 annotates 'diss_length: null # = 2 * fft_length (default)' (epsilon) and :93 '# = 3 * fft_length (default)' (chi). Actual default is 4*fft_length everywhere: dissipation.py:101-102 'diss_length = 4 * fft_length', chi_io.py:68-69 same, pipeline.py:1343 '4 * eps_cfg.get(fft_length,256)', :1346 '4 * chi_cfg.get(fft_length,512)'. Template config.py:589 and :612 correctly say 'null = 4 * fft_length'. So epsilon window is 4*256=1024 samp=2 s (not the commented 2x=1 s) and chi 4*512=2048 samp=4 s (not 3x=3 s). No wrong numbers (null still resolves to 4x in code), but the checked-in publication config comment misstates the processing window a methods reader would cite. The finding's own '0.5 s' arithmetic is imprecise, but the core 2x/3x-vs-4x claim is correct -> minor.

confirmed_findings.title: docs/perturb/pipeline.md lists the per-file sub-stages in an order that contradicts the code (CT alignment and FP07 calibration listed AFTER the products they feed)
confirmed_findings.claim: pipeline.md numbers the sub-stages as 2. CTD time-binning, 3. Profile extraction, 4. FP07 calibration, 5. Impact is narrow — only on a pandas upgrade combined with cache reuse — so minor/borderline-nit, not major.

confirmed_findings.title: chi_mathematics.md Section 7 still documents the pre-PR#79 V_resolved band (integral from 0) and misstates the MLE low-k fit cutoff
confirmed_findings.claim: Section 7 (Method 2a) line 410 states 'V_resolved = integral_0^{K_max_fit} of Phi_model * |H(k)|^2' but the code (post audit-fix #98 in PR #79) integrates V_resolved from K_fit_low (chi.py:444-453) precisely because the 0-based band was a documented chi-low bias, and line 386 says the MLE low-k cutoff is 'first wavenumber bin above zero' whereas the fit mask (chi.py:170-173 via :342) requires Phi_obs > 2*Phi_noise at every bin — a reader implementing the correction from the doc would reproduce the pre-fix biased behavior.
confirmed_findings.evidence: docs/chi_mathematics.md:410 vs chi.py:444-453 (K_fit_low = K[fit_mask][0]; _variance_correction(..., K_min=K_fit_low) with an explicit comment that the K_min=0 default 'inflat[ed] V_resolved and bias[ed] chi low ... (audit finding)'); _variance_correction masks (K_fine >= K_min) & (K_fine <= min(K_max,K_upper)) at chi.py:125. docs line 386 vs _valid_wavenumber_mask chi.py:170-173 (above_noise & K>0 & K<=K_AA, fallback to all K>0 only when fewer than 6 bins pass). Method 1's Step 5 (doc :314-318) correctly keeps 0 — chi.py:261 uses default K_min=0 there. Prior audit #98 recorded the CODE bug (now fixed); the doc lag is new.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: Compare docs/chi_mathematics.md:386 and :406-411 with src/odas_tpw/chi/chi.py:170-174, :342, :444-453 at HEAD; confirm the code passes K_min=K[fit_mask][0] while the doc writes lower limit 0.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Change line 410 to 'V_resolved = integral_{K_fit_low}^{K_max_fit}' noting the resolved-band span matches the obs_var band (audit fix #98); reword line 386 to 'first wavenumber bin above the 2x-noise criterion (fallback to all k in (0, K_AA] when fewer than 6 bins qualify)'.
merged_from: chi_mathematics.md Method 2a still documents V_resolved integrated from 0 — the exact pre-fix behavior audit #98 identified as biasing chi low

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed doc/code mismatch. chi_mathematics.md:410 writes 'V_resolved = integral_0^{K_max_fit} Phi_model*|H|^2', but Method 2a code passes K_min=K_fit_low=float(K[fit_mask][0]) (chi.py:444-453) into _variance_correction, whose mask is (K_fine>=K_min)&(K_fine<=min(K_max,K_upper)) (chi.py:125). The code comment (chi.py:441-443) explicitly states the prior K_min=0 default 'inflat[ed] V_resolved and bias[ed] chi low for low-epsilon windows (audit finding).' So the doc still describes the superseded, biased 0-based band; a reader re-implementing from it reproduces the pre-fix low bias. Secondary: doc:386 'Low-k cutoff: first wavenumber bin above zero' is imprecise vs the actual above-2*noise fit mask (_valid_wavenumber_mask chi.py:170-173, min_points=6 at chi.py:342). Bounded scientific impact (modest chi low-bias). Minor.

confirmed_findings.title: epsilon_mathematics.md claims the polynomial spectral-minimum fit order 'is decreased until one is found' — no such retry loop exists in the code
confirmed_findings.claim: Section 7 Step 4 line 369 states 'If no suitable minimum is found (polynomial fit order is decreased until one is found, down to order 3), K_max = K_limit', but _variance_method performs exactly ONE polyfit at fit_order_eff = min(max(fit_order,3),8) and falls straight back to K_95 when no qualifying root exists — the order-decreasing retry is ODAS get_diss_odas.m behavior, not this port.
confirmed_findings.evidence: scor160/l4.py:522-538: single np.polyfit, roots filtered, 'K_limit_log = np.min(roots) if len(roots) > 0 else np.log10(K_95)'; grep -rn 'fit_order' shows no decrement loop. Default path (fit_order=3) numerically unaffected; users setting fit_order 4-8 per the documented range expect fallback behavior that does not exist.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: Read scor160/l4.py:522-542; grep -rn 'fit_order' src/odas_tpw/scor160/l4.py src/odas_tpw/rsi/dissipation.py.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Reword line 369 ('single polynomial fit, order clamped 3-8; fallback min(K_95, K_AA) clipped to [7,150] cpm') or implement the ODAS retry.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed: no such retry loop exists. epsilon_mathematics.md:369 claims 'polynomial fit order is decreased until one is found, down to order 3.' l4.py:522 sets a single fit_order_eff=min(max(fit_order,3),8); l4.py:525-538 does exactly one np.polyfit, filters roots, and sets K_limit_log=np.min(roots) if any else np.log10(K_95). grep of fit_order in l4.py shows only l4.py:92/121/227/312/333/413/488/522/525/527 — no decrement/while loop. So when no qualifying minimum is found it falls straight to K_95; the order-decreasing retry is not present. Default fit_order=3 is numerically unaffected (nothing to decrement to); users setting fit_order 4-8 per the documented range get behavior differing from the doc. Doc/code mismatch touching the K_max integration limit, bounded impact. Minor.

confirmed_findings.title: chi_mathematics.md says chi-window viscosity 'is computed from visc35(T_mean)' — the default rsi-tpw pipeline uses salinity/pressure-aware visc(T, S_measured, P)
confirmed_findings.claim: Section 10 line 556 states 'Kinematic viscosity nu is computed from visc35(T_mean)', but on the default rsi-tpw pipeline path (and perturb with chi.salinity='measured') per-sample practical salinity from the profile's own JAC C/T is resolved first and nu = visc(T_mean, S_measured, P_mean) is used; visc35 applies only when no conductivity and no user salinity exist.
confirmed_findings.evidence: rsi/pipeline.py:96-118 _resolve_salinity (measured > user-supplied > 35) and :419 applies it to chi; chi/l3_chi.py:178-195 uses visc(T,S,P) when salinity is not None else visc35. ARCTERX carries JAC_C/JAC_T, so the measured branch is the production default; ARCHITECTURE.md:406-408 itself says chi-viscosity salinity was 'reconciled ... (measured JAC C/T)'.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: Read docs/chi_mathematics.md:552-558, then l3_chi.py:178-195 and rsi/pipeline.py:96-118,416-426.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Change line 556: 'nu is computed from visc(T_mean, S, P_mean) when salinity is available (measured JAC C/T on the pipeline path, or user-supplied), falling back to visc35(T_mean) otherwise.'

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed doc inaccuracy for the production default. chi_mathematics.md:556 states unconditionally 'Kinematic viscosity nu is computed from visc35(T_mean).' Actual default rsi-tpw path: pipeline.py:419 calls _resolve_salinity which returns per-sample measured JAC C/T salinity first (pipeline.py:114-115) and passes it to process_l3_chi, which uses visc(T_mean,S_mean,P_mean) whenever salinity is not None (l3_chi.py:178-192) and visc35 only in the None fallback (l3_chi.py:193-197). Campaign VMP-250 carries JAC_C/JAC_T, so the measured branch is production default; ARCHITECTURE.md separately documents this reconciliation. Not a prior-audit duplicate (audit #2 concerned epsilon-side visc35 code, now fixed). Viscosity differs only a few percent and chi is weakly nu-sensitive, so bounded impact. Minor doc-inconsistency.

confirmed_findings.title: rsi-tpw pipeline --salinity is silently ignored on conductivity-equipped instruments, contradicting the documented 'Salinity [PSU] for viscosity' semantics
confirmed_findings.claim: docs/rsi-tpw/cli.md:248 (and the CLI --help) documents 'rsi-tpw pipeline --salinity FLOAT' as 'Salinity [PSU] for viscosity (default: 35, fixed S)', but run_pipeline's _resolve_salinity prefers MEASURED JAC C/T salinity over the user value, so on any conductivity-equipped instrument (including the campaign VMP-250) a user-passed --salinity has no effect on the pipeline's epsilon/chi/N2 viscosity, with no warning.
confirmed_findings.evidence: rsi/pipeline.py:114-118: measured salinity returned before the user argument is consulted; rsi/cli.py:565 passes args.salinity into run_pipeline. Neither cli.md:236-248 nor the argparse help mentions the measured-salinity override; docs/rsi-tpw/configuration.md:66 implies the user value is used.
confirmed_findings.how_to_verify: Read rsi/pipeline.py:96-118 (preference docstring) and rsi/cli.py:565; compare docs/rsi-tpw/cli.md:248 and 'rsi-tpw pipeline --help' with PYTHONPATH set.
confirmed_findings.suggested_fix: Document the precedence in cli.md/configuration.md and argparse help, or invert precedence so an explicit user value wins with a warning.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed. Pipeline subcommand --salinity help (cli.py:978-981, set_defaults func=_cmd_pipeline) reads 'Salinity [PSU] for viscosity (default: 35, fixed S)', matching cli.md:248. But _cmd_pipeline passes salinity=args.salinity into run_pipeline (cli.py:565), and _resolve_salinity returns measured JAC C/T salinity BEFORE the user value is consulted (pipeline.py:114-118) with no warning. On conductivity-equipped instruments (campaign VMP-250 has JAC_C/JAC_T) the user's --salinity is silently ignored for chi/epsilon/N2 viscosity, contradicting the documented 'fixed S' semantics. Contrast: the mixing subcommand's own --salinity help (cli.py:1157) correctly states 'default: measured from JAC C/T, else 35', so the pipeline/eps/chi help texts are the stale ones. No wrong published number (measured S is more correct), but the explicit override is silently dropped. Minor misleading-doc/foot-gun.

confirmed_findings.title: CLI reference tables omit the shipped 'rsi-tpw ml' mixing viewer subcommand
confirmed_findings.claim: The 'All commands' table in docs/rsi-tpw/cli.md (lines 11-24) and the CLI table in docs/DATA_FLOW.md (lines 16-27) omit the 'ml' (Interactive mixing viewer) subcommand that the CLI registers and lists in its own --help (DATA_FLOW.md additionally omits patch-config/patch-template), so the mixing viewer for N2/dTdz/K_T/Gamma/K_rho is undiscoverable from the reference docs.
